paper-entry.title: [Paper Note] Exploring MLLM-Diffusion Information Transfer with MetaCanvas, Han Lin+, arXiv'25, 2025.12
paper-entry.tags: ComputerVision, NLP, Temporal, LanguageModel, MultiModal, DiffusionModel, PEFT(Adaptor/LoRA), SpatialUnderstanding
paper-entry.issue-body: # URL - https://arxiv.org/abs/2512.11464 # Authors - Han Lin - Xichen Pan - Ziqi Huang - Ji Hou - Jialiang Wang - Weifeng Chen - Zecheng He - Felix Juefei-Xu - Junzhe Sun - Zhipeng Fan - Ali Thabet - Mohit Bansal - Chu Wang # Abstract - Multimodal learning has rapidly advanced visual understanding, largely via multimodal large language models (MLLMs) that use powerful LLMs as cognitive cores. In visual generation, however, these powerful core models are typically reduced to global text encoders for diffusion models, leaving most of their reasoning and planning ability unused. This creates a gap: current multimodal LLMs can parse complex layouts, attributes, and knowledge-intensive scenes, yet struggle to generate images or videos with equally precise and structured control. We propose MetaCanvas, a lightweight framework that lets MLLMs reason and plan directly in spatial and spatiotemporal latent spaces and interface tightly with diffusion generators. We empirically implement MetaCanvas on three different diffusion backbones and evaluate it across six tasks, including text-to-image generation, text/image-to-video generation, image/video editing, and in-context video generation, each requiring precise layouts, robust attribute binding, and reasoning-intensive control. MetaCanvas consistently outperforms global-conditioning baselines, suggesting that treating MLLMs as latent-space planners is a promising direction for narrowing the gap between multimodal understanding and generation. # Translation (by gpt-4o-mini) - гғһгғ«гғҒгғўгғјгғҖгғ«еӯҰзҝ’гҒҜгҖҒдё»гҒ«еј·еҠӣгҒӘеӨ§иҰҸжЁЎиЁҖиӘһгғўгғҮгғ«пјҲLLMsпјүгӮ’иӘҚзҹҘгӮігӮўгҒЁгҒ—гҒҰдҪҝз”ЁгҒҷгӮӢгғһгғ«гғҒгғўгғјгғҖгғ«еӨ§иҰҸжЁЎиЁҖиӘһгғўгғҮгғ«пјҲMLLMsпјүгҒ«гӮҲгҒЈгҒҰгҖҒиҰ–иҰҡзҗҶи§ЈгӮ’жҖҘйҖҹгҒ«йҖІеұ•гҒ•гҒӣгҒҰгҒҚгҒҹгҖӮгҒ—гҒӢгҒ—гҖҒиҰ–иҰҡз”ҹжҲҗгҒ«гҒҠгҒ„гҒҰгҖҒгҒ“гӮҢгӮүгҒ®еј·еҠӣгҒӘгӮігӮўгғўгғҮгғ«гҒҜйҖҡеёёгҖҒжӢЎж•ЈгғўгғҮгғ«гҒ®гҒҹгӮҒгҒ®гӮ°гғӯгғјгғҗгғ«гғҶгӮӯгӮ№гғҲгӮЁгғігӮігғјгғҖгғјгҒ«йӮ„е…ғгҒ•гӮҢгҒҰгҒҠгӮҠгҖҒгҒқгҒ®жҺЁи«–иғҪеҠӣгӮ„иЁҲз”»иғҪеҠӣгҒ®еӨ§йғЁеҲҶгҒҢжңӘдҪҝз”ЁгҒ®гҒҫгҒҫгҒЁгҒӘгҒЈгҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгҖӮгҒ“гҒ®гҒҹгӮҒгҖҒзҸҫеңЁгҒ®гғһгғ«гғҒгғўгғјгғҖгғ«LLMsгҒҜиӨҮйӣ‘гҒӘгғ¬гӮӨгӮўгӮҰгғҲгҖҒеұһжҖ§гҖҒзҹҘиӯҳйӣҶзҙ„зҡ„гҒӘгӮ·гғјгғігӮ’и§ЈжһҗгҒҷгӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгҒҜгҒ§гҒҚгӮӢгҒҢгҖҒеҗҢж§ҳгҒ«жӯЈзўәгҒ§ж§ӢйҖ еҢ–гҒ•гӮҢгҒҹеҲ¶еҫЎгӮ’жҢҒгҒЈгҒҰз”»еғҸгӮ„еӢ•з”»гӮ’з”ҹжҲҗгҒҷгӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгҒ«гҒҜиӢҰеҠҙгҒ—гҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгҖӮгҒқгҒ“гҒ§гҖҒз§ҒгҒҹгҒЎгҒҜMetaCanvasгҒЁгҒ„гҒҶи»ҪйҮҸгғ•гғ¬гғјгғ гғҜгғјгӮҜгӮ’жҸҗжЎҲгҒҷгӮӢгҖӮгҒ“гӮҢгҒ«гӮҲгӮҠгҖҒMLLMsгҒҜз©әй–“зҡ„гҒҠгӮҲгҒіжҷӮз©әй–“зҡ„жҪңеңЁз©әй–“гҒ§зӣҙжҺҘжҺЁи«–гҒЁиЁҲз”»гӮ’иЎҢгҒ„гҖҒжӢЎж•Јз”ҹжҲҗеҷЁгҒЁеҜҶжҺҘгҒ«гӮӨгғігӮҝгғјгғ•гӮ§гғјгӮ№гӮ’жҢҒгҒӨгҒ“гҒЁгҒҢгҒ§гҒҚгӮӢгҖӮз§ҒгҒҹгҒЎгҒҜгҖҒMetaCanvasгӮ’3гҒӨгҒ®з•°гҒӘгӮӢжӢЎж•ЈгғҗгғғгӮҜгғңгғјгғідёҠгҒ§е®ҹиЈ…гҒ—гҖҒгғҶгӮӯгӮ№гғҲгҒӢгӮүз”»еғҸз”ҹжҲҗгҖҒгғҶгӮӯгӮ№гғҲ/з”»еғҸгҒӢгӮүеӢ•з”»з”ҹжҲҗгҖҒз”»еғҸ/еӢ•з”»з·ЁйӣҶгҖҒж–Үи„ҲеҶ…еӢ•з”»з”ҹжҲҗгҒӘгҒ©гҖҒжӯЈзўәгҒӘгғ¬гӮӨгӮўгӮҰгғҲгҖҒе …зүўгҒӘеұһжҖ§гғҗгӮӨгғігғҮгӮЈгғігӮ°гҖҒжҺЁи«–йӣҶзҙ„зҡ„гҒӘеҲ¶еҫЎгӮ’еҝ…иҰҒгҒЁгҒҷгӮӢ6гҒӨгҒ®гӮҝгӮ№гӮҜгҒ§и©•дҫЎгҒ—гҒҹгҖӮMetaCanvasгҒҜеёёгҒ«гӮ°гғӯгғјгғҗгғ«жқЎд»¶д»ҳгҒ‘гҒ®гғҷгғјгӮ№гғ©гӮӨгғігӮ’дёҠеӣһгӮҠгҖҒMLLMsгӮ’жҪңеңЁз©әй–“гҒ®гғ—гғ©гғігғҠгғјгҒЁгҒ—гҒҰжүұгҒҶгҒ“гҒЁгҒҢгҖҒгғһгғ«гғҒгғўгғјгғҖгғ«зҗҶи§ЈгҒЁз”ҹжҲҗгҒ®гӮ®гғЈгғғгғ—гӮ’зӢӯгӮҒгӮӢжңүжңӣгҒӘж–№еҗ‘жҖ§гҒ§гҒӮгӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгӮ’зӨәе”ҶгҒ—гҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгҖӮ # Summary (by gpt-4o-mini) - MetaCanvasгҒЁгҒ„гҒҶи»ҪйҮҸгғ•гғ¬гғјгғ гғҜгғјгӮҜгӮ’жҸҗжЎҲгҒ—гҖҒгғһгғ«гғҒгғўгғјгғҖгғ«еӨ§иҰҸжЁЎиЁҖиӘһгғўгғҮгғ«пјҲMLLMsпјүгҒҢз©әй–“зҡ„гҒҠгӮҲгҒіжҷӮз©әй–“зҡ„жҪңеңЁз©әй–“гҒ§зӣҙжҺҘжҺЁи«–гҒЁиЁҲз”»гӮ’иЎҢгҒҲгӮӢгӮҲгҒҶгҒ«гҒҷгӮӢгҖӮгҒ“гӮҢгҒ«гӮҲгӮҠгҖҒз”»еғҸгӮ„еӢ•з”»з”ҹжҲҗгҒ«гҒҠгҒ„гҒҰжӯЈзўәгҒӘеҲ¶еҫЎгӮ’е®ҹзҸҫгҖӮ6гҒӨгҒ®гӮҝгӮ№гӮҜгҒ§и©•дҫЎгҒ—гҒҹзөҗжһңгҖҒMetaCanvasгҒҜгӮ°гғӯгғјгғҗгғ«жқЎд»¶д»ҳгҒ‘гҒ®гғҷгғјгӮ№гғ©гӮӨгғігӮ’еёёгҒ«дёҠеӣһгӮҠгҖҒMLLMsгӮ’жҪңеңЁз©әй–“гҒ®гғ—гғ©гғігғҠгғјгҒЁгҒ—гҒҰжүұгҒҶгҒ“гҒЁгҒҢжңүжңӣгҒ§гҒӮгӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгӮ’зӨәгҒ—гҒҹгҖӮ

paper-entry.title: [Paper Note] VLIC: Vision-Language Models As Perceptual Judges for Human-Aligned Image Compression, Kyle Sargent+, arXiv'25, 2025.12
paper-entry.tags: ComputerVision, Alignment, DiffusionModel, LLM-as-a-Judge, DPO, PostTraining, 2D (Image), One-Line Notes, AutoEncoder
paper-entry.issue-body: # URL - https://arxiv.org/abs/2512.15701 # Authors - Kyle Sargent - Ruiqi Gao - Philipp Henzler - Charles Herrmann - Aleksander Holynski - Li Fei-Fei - Jiajun Wu - Jason Zhang # Abstract - Evaluations of image compression performance which include human preferences have generally found that naive distortion functions such as MSE are insufficiently aligned to human perception. In order to align compression models to human perception, prior work has employed differentiable perceptual losses consisting of neural networks calibrated on large-scale datasets of human psycho-visual judgments. We show that, surprisingly, state-of-the-art vision-language models (VLMs) can replicate binary human two-alternative forced choice (2AFC) judgments zero-shot when asked to reason about the differences between pairs of images. Motivated to exploit the powerful zero-shot visual reasoning capabilities of VLMs, we propose Vision-Language Models for Image Compression (VLIC), a diffusion-based image compression system designed to be post-trained with binary VLM judgments. VLIC leverages existing techniques for diffusion model post-training with preferences, rather than distilling the VLM judgments into a separate perceptual loss network. We show that calibrating this system on VLM judgments produces competitive or state-of-the-art performance on human-aligned visual compression depending on the dataset, according to perceptual metrics and large-scale user studies. We additionally conduct an extensive analysis of the VLM-based reward design and training procedure and share important insights. More visuals are available at https://kylesargent.github.io/vlic # Translation (by gpt-4o-mini) - з”»еғҸең§зё®жҖ§иғҪгҒ®и©•дҫЎгҒ«гҒҠгҒ„гҒҰгҖҒдәәй–“гҒ®еҘҪгҒҝгӮ’иҖғж…®гҒ—гҒҹгӮӮгҒ®гҒҜгҖҒдёҖиҲ¬зҡ„гҒ«MSEгҒ®гӮҲгҒҶгҒӘеҚҳзҙ”гҒӘжӯӘгҒҝй–ўж•°гҒҢдәәй–“гҒ®зҹҘиҰҡгҒЁеҚҒеҲҶгҒ«дёҖиҮҙгҒ—гҒҰгҒ„гҒӘгҒ„гҒ“гҒЁгҒҢгӮҸгҒӢгҒЈгҒҰгҒ„гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮең§зё®гғўгғҮгғ«гӮ’дәәй–“гҒ®зҹҘиҰҡгҒ«еҗҲгӮҸгҒӣгӮӢгҒҹгӮҒгҒ«гҖҒгҒ“гӮҢгҒҫгҒ§гҒ®з ”з©¶гҒ§гҒҜгҖҒеӨ§иҰҸжЁЎгҒӘдәәй–“гҒ®еҝғзҗҶиҰ–иҰҡзҡ„еҲӨж–ӯгғҮгғјгӮҝгӮ»гғғгғҲгҒ«еҹәгҒҘгҒ„гҒҰиӘҝж•ҙгҒ•гӮҢгҒҹгғӢгғҘгғјгғ©гғ«гғҚгғғгғҲгғҜгғјгӮҜгҒӢгӮүгҒӘгӮӢеҫ®еҲҶеҸҜиғҪгҒӘзҹҘиҰҡжҗҚеӨұгҒҢз”ЁгҒ„гӮүгӮҢгҒҰгҒҚгҒҫгҒ—гҒҹгҖӮй©ҡгҒҸгҒ№гҒҚгҒ“гҒЁгҒ«гҖҒжңҖе…Ҳз«ҜгҒ®иҰ–иҰҡ-иЁҖиӘһгғўгғҮгғ«пјҲVLMпјүгҒҜгҖҒз”»еғҸгҒ®гғҡгӮўй–“гҒ®йҒ•гҒ„гҒ«гҒӨгҒ„гҒҰжҺЁи«–гҒҷгӮӢгӮҲгҒҶгҒ«жұӮгӮҒгӮүгӮҢгҒҹгҒЁгҒҚгҒ«гҖҒгӮјгғӯгӮ·гғ§гғғгғҲгҒ§дәәй–“гҒ®дәҢиҖ…жҠһдёҖеј·еҲ¶йҒёжҠһпјҲ2AFCпјүеҲӨж–ӯгӮ’еҶҚзҸҫгҒ§гҒҚгӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгӮ’зӨәгҒ—гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮгҒ“гҒ®VLMгҒ®еј·еҠӣгҒӘгӮјгғӯгӮ·гғ§гғғгғҲиҰ–иҰҡжҺЁи«–иғҪеҠӣгӮ’жҙ»з”ЁгҒҷгӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгӮ’зӣ®зҡ„гҒЁгҒ—гҒҰгҖҒжҲ‘гҖ…гҒҜз”»еғҸең§зё®гҒ®гҒҹгӮҒгҒ®иҰ–иҰҡ-иЁҖиӘһгғўгғҮгғ«пјҲVLICпјүгӮ’жҸҗжЎҲгҒ—гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮгҒ“гӮҢгҒҜгҖҒгғҗгӮӨгғҠгғӘVLMеҲӨж–ӯгҒ§еҫҢеҮҰзҗҶгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢгӮҲгҒҶгҒ«иЁӯиЁҲгҒ•гӮҢгҒҹжӢЎж•ЈгғҷгғјгӮ№гҒ®з”»еғҸең§зё®гӮ·гӮ№гғҶгғ гҒ§гҒҷгҖӮVLICгҒҜгҖҒVLMеҲӨж–ӯгӮ’еҲҘгҒ®зҹҘиҰҡжҗҚеӨұгғҚгғғгғҲгғҜгғјгӮҜгҒ«и’ёз•ҷгҒҷгӮӢгҒ®гҒ§гҒҜгҒӘгҒҸгҖҒеҘҪгҒҝгҒ«еҹәгҒҘгҒҸжӢЎж•ЈгғўгғҮгғ«гҒ®еҫҢеҮҰзҗҶгҒ®гҒҹгӮҒгҒ®ж—ўеӯҳгҒ®жҠҖиЎ“гӮ’жҙ»з”ЁгҒ—гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮгҒ“гҒ®гӮ·гӮ№гғҶгғ гӮ’VLMеҲӨж–ӯгҒ«еҹәгҒҘгҒ„гҒҰиӘҝж•ҙгҒҷгӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгҒ§гҖҒгғҮгғјгӮҝгӮ»гғғгғҲгҒ«еҝңгҒҳгҒҰдәәй–“гҒ«еҗҲгӮҸгҒӣгҒҹиҰ–иҰҡең§зё®гҒ«гҒҠгҒ„гҒҰз«¶дәүеҠӣгҒ®гҒӮгӮӢгҖҒгҒҫгҒҹгҒҜжңҖе…Ҳз«ҜгҒ®жҖ§иғҪгӮ’зҷәжҸ®гҒҷгӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгӮ’зӨәгҒ—гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮгҒ•гӮүгҒ«гҖҒVLMгғҷгғјгӮ№гҒ®е ұй…¬иЁӯиЁҲгҒЁгғҲгғ¬гғјгғӢгғігӮ°жүӢй ҶгҒ«гҒӨгҒ„гҒҰгҒ®еәғзҜ„гҒӘеҲҶжһҗгӮ’иЎҢгҒ„гҖҒйҮҚиҰҒгҒӘжҙһеҜҹгӮ’е…ұжңүгҒ—гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮи©ізҙ°гҒӘгғ“гӮёгғҘгӮўгғ«гҒҜгҖҒhttps://kylesargent.github.io/vlic гҒ§гҒ”иҰ§гҒ„гҒҹгҒ гҒ‘гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮ # Summary (by gpt-4o-mini) - дәәй–“гҒ®еҘҪгҒҝгҒ«еҹәгҒҘгҒҸз”»еғҸең§зё®гҒ®гҒҹгӮҒгҒ«гҖҒиҰ–иҰҡ-иЁҖиӘһгғўгғҮгғ«пјҲVLMпјүгӮ’жҙ»з”ЁгҒ—гҒҹж–°гҒ—гҒ„гӮ·гӮ№гғҶгғ VLICгӮ’жҸҗжЎҲгҖӮVLICгҒҜгҖҒгғҗгӮӨгғҠгғӘVLMеҲӨж–ӯгӮ’з”ЁгҒ„гҒҹжӢЎж•ЈгғҷгғјгӮ№гҒ®з”»еғҸең§зё®гӮ·гӮ№гғҶгғ гҒ§гҖҒеҫ“жқҘгҒ®зҹҘиҰҡжҗҚеӨұгғҚгғғгғҲгғҜгғјгӮҜгӮ’и’ёз•ҷгҒҷгӮӢгҒ®гҒ§гҒҜгҒӘгҒҸгҖҒж—ўеӯҳжҠҖиЎ“гӮ’жҙ»з”ЁгҖӮгҒ“гӮҢгҒ«гӮҲгӮҠгҖҒгғҮгғјгӮҝгӮ»гғғгғҲгҒ«еҝңгҒҳгҒҹз«¶дәүеҠӣгҒ®гҒӮгӮӢжҖ§иғҪгӮ’е®ҹзҸҫгҖӮVLMгғҷгғјгӮ№гҒ®е ұй…¬иЁӯиЁҲгҒЁгғҲгғ¬гғјгғӢгғігӮ°жүӢй ҶгҒ«гҒӨгҒ„гҒҰгӮӮеҲҶжһҗгӮ’иЎҢгҒ„гҖҒйҮҚиҰҒгҒӘжҙһеҜҹгӮ’жҸҗдҫӣгҖӮ

maintainer-comment-text.p:
гҒ–гҒЈгҒҸгӮҠиЁҖгҒҶгҒЁгҖҒеҗҢгҒҳжҪңеңЁиЎЁзҸҫгҒ«еҜҫгҒ—гҒҰз•°гҒӘгӮӢ2гҒӨгҒ®гғҺгӮӨгӮәгӮ·гғјгғүгҒ«еҜҫгҒ—гҒҰз”»еғҸгӮ’з”ҹжҲҗгҒ—гҖҒVLM-as-a-JudgeгӮ’з”ЁгҒ„гҒҰдәәй–“гҒ®зҹҘиҰҡзҡ„гҒӘеҘҪгҒҝгҒ«иҝ‘гҒ„гӮ№гӮігӮўгӮ’еҫ—гӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгҒ§гҖҒpreferenceгғҡгӮўгӮ’еҗҲжҲҗгҖӮгҒ“гҒ®жғ…е ұгҒ«еҹәгҒҘгҒ„гҒҰDiffusion DPOгҒЁе‘јгҒ°гӮҢгӮӢDPOгҒ®diffusionгғўгғҮгғ«зүҲгӮ’з”ЁгҒ„гҒҰDiffusion autoencoderгӮ’еӯҰзҝ’гҒҷгӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгҒ§гҖҒгӮҲгӮҠдәәй–“гҒ®зҹҘиҰҡзҡ„гҒӘеҲӨж–ӯгҒ«иҝ‘гҒ„з”»еғҸең§зё®гғ»еҫ©е…ғйҒҺзЁӢгӮ’еӯҰзҝ’гҒҷгӮӢгҖҒгҒЁгҒ„гҒҶгӮҲгҒҶгҒӘи©ұгҒЈгҒҪгҒ„гҖӮ
е®ҹйҡӣгҒ®гӮөгғігғ—гғ«гӮ’иҰӢгӮӢгҒЁгҖҒжҳҺгӮүгҒӢгҒ«гғҶгӮӯгӮ№гғҲгҒ®еҙ©гӮҢгҒҢгҒӘгҒҸгҒӘгҒЈгҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгҒ®гҒҢгӮҸгҒӢгӮӢгҖӮ

paper-entry.title: [Paper Note] Diffusion as Shader: 3D-aware Video Diffusion for Versatile Video Generation Control, Zekai Gu+, SIGGRAPH'25, 2025.01
paper-entry.tags: ComputerVision, Controllable, DiffusionModel, SIGGRAPH, VideoGeneration/Understandings
paper-entry.issue-body: # URL - https://arxiv.org/abs/2501.03847 # Authors - Zekai Gu - Rui Yan - Jiahao Lu - Peng Li - Zhiyang Dou - Chenyang Si - Zhen Dong - Qifeng Liu - Cheng Lin - Ziwei Liu - Wenping Wang - Yuan Liu # Abstract - Diffusion models have demonstrated impressive performance in generating high-quality videos from text prompts or images. However, precise control over the video generation process, such as camera manipulation or content editing, remains a significant challenge. Existing methods for controlled video generation are typically limited to a single control type, lacking the flexibility to handle diverse control demands. In this paper, we introduce Diffusion as Shader (DaS), a novel approach that supports multiple video control tasks within a unified architecture. Our key insight is that achieving versatile video control necessitates leveraging 3D control signals, as videos are fundamentally 2D renderings of dynamic 3D content. Unlike prior methods limited to 2D control signals, DaS leverages 3D tracking videos as control inputs, making the video diffusion process inherently 3D-aware. This innovation allows DaS to achieve a wide range of video controls by simply manipulating the 3D tracking videos. A further advantage of using 3D tracking videos is their ability to effectively link frames, significantly enhancing the temporal consistency of the generated videos. With just 3 days of fine-tuning on 8 H800 GPUs using less than 10k videos, DaS demonstrates strong control capabilities across diverse tasks, including mesh-to-video generation, camera control, motion transfer, and object manipulation. # Translation (by gpt-4o-mini) - жӢЎж•ЈгғўгғҮгғ«гҒҜгҖҒгғҶгӮӯгӮ№гғҲгғ—гғӯгғігғ—гғҲгӮ„з”»еғҸгҒӢгӮүй«ҳе“ҒиіӘгҒӘеӢ•з”»гӮ’з”ҹжҲҗгҒҷгӮӢдёҠгҒ§еҚ°иұЎзҡ„гҒӘжҖ§иғҪгӮ’зӨәгҒ—гҒҰгҒ„гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮгҒ—гҒӢгҒ—гҖҒгӮ«гғЎгғ©ж“ҚдҪңгӮ„гӮігғігғҶгғігғ„з·ЁйӣҶгҒӘгҒ©гҖҒеӢ•з”»з”ҹжҲҗгғ—гғӯгӮ»гӮ№гҒ«еҜҫгҒҷгӮӢжӯЈзўәгҒӘеҲ¶еҫЎгҒҜдҫқз„¶гҒЁгҒ—гҒҰеӨ§гҒҚгҒӘиӘІйЎҢгҒ§гҒҷгҖӮж—ўеӯҳгҒ®еҲ¶еҫЎгҒ•гӮҢгҒҹеӢ•з”»з”ҹжҲҗжүӢжі•гҒҜйҖҡеёёгҖҒеҚҳдёҖгҒ®еҲ¶еҫЎгӮҝгӮӨгғ—гҒ«еҲ¶йҷҗгҒ•гӮҢгҒҰгҒҠгӮҠгҖҒеӨҡж§ҳгҒӘеҲ¶еҫЎиҰҒжұӮгҒ«еҜҫеҝңгҒҷгӮӢжҹ”и»ҹжҖ§гҒ«ж¬ гҒ‘гҒҰгҒ„гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮжң¬и«–ж–ҮгҒ§гҒҜгҖҒиӨҮж•°гҒ®еӢ•з”»еҲ¶еҫЎгӮҝгӮ№гӮҜгӮ’зөұдёҖгҒ•гӮҢгҒҹгӮўгғјгӮӯгғҶгӮҜгғҒгғЈеҶ…гҒ§гӮөгғқгғјгғҲгҒҷгӮӢж–°гҒ—гҒ„гӮўгғ—гғӯгғјгғҒгҖҢDiffusion as ShaderпјҲDaSпјүгҖҚгӮ’зҙ№д»ӢгҒ—гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮжҲ‘гҖ…гҒ®йҮҚиҰҒгҒӘжҙһеҜҹгҒҜгҖҒеӨҡж§ҳгҒӘеӢ•з”»еҲ¶еҫЎгӮ’е®ҹзҸҫгҒҷгӮӢгҒҹгӮҒгҒ«гҒҜ3DеҲ¶еҫЎдҝЎеҸ·гӮ’жҙ»з”ЁгҒҷгӮӢеҝ…иҰҒгҒҢгҒӮгӮӢгҒЁгҒ„гҒҶгҒ“гҒЁгҒ§гҒҷгҖӮгҒӘгҒңгҒӘгӮүгҖҒеӢ•з”»гҒҜжң¬иіӘзҡ„гҒ«еӢ•зҡ„гҒӘ3DгӮігғігғҶгғігғ„гҒ®2Dгғ¬гғігғҖгғӘгғігӮ°гҒ гҒӢгӮүгҒ§гҒҷгҖӮеҫ“жқҘгҒ®2DеҲ¶еҫЎдҝЎеҸ·гҒ«еҲ¶йҷҗгҒ•гӮҢгҒҹжүӢжі•гҒЁгҒҜз•°гҒӘгӮҠгҖҒDaSгҒҜ3DгғҲгғ©гғғгӮӯгғігӮ°еӢ•з”»гӮ’еҲ¶еҫЎе…ҘеҠӣгҒЁгҒ—гҒҰжҙ»з”ЁгҒ—гҖҒеӢ•з”»гҒ®жӢЎж•Јгғ—гғӯгӮ»гӮ№гӮ’жң¬иіӘзҡ„гҒ«3DгҒ«ж„ҸиӯҳгҒ•гҒӣгҒҫгҒҷгҖӮгҒ“гҒ®йқ©ж–°гҒ«гӮҲгӮҠгҖҒDaSгҒҜ3DгғҲгғ©гғғгӮӯгғігӮ°еӢ•з”»гӮ’еҚҳгҒ«ж“ҚдҪңгҒҷгӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгҒ§е№…еәғгҒ„еӢ•з”»еҲ¶еҫЎгӮ’е®ҹзҸҫгҒ—гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮгҒ•гӮүгҒ«гҖҒ3DгғҲгғ©гғғгӮӯгғігӮ°еӢ•з”»гӮ’дҪҝз”ЁгҒҷгӮӢеҲ©зӮ№гҒҜгҖҒгғ•гғ¬гғјгғ гӮ’еҠ№жһңзҡ„гҒ«гғӘгғігӮҜгҒ§гҒҚгӮӢгҒҹгӮҒгҖҒз”ҹжҲҗгҒ•гӮҢгҒҹеӢ•з”»гҒ®жҷӮй–“зҡ„дёҖиІ«жҖ§гҒҢеӨ§е№…гҒ«еҗ‘дёҠгҒҷгӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгҒ§гҒҷгҖӮ8еҸ°гҒ®H800 GPUгӮ’дҪҝз”ЁгҒ—гҖҒ1дёҮжң¬жңӘжәҖгҒ®еӢ•з”»гҒ§гӮҸгҒҡгҒӢ3ж—Ҙй–“гҒ®гғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғігғҒгғҘгғјгғӢгғігӮ°гӮ’иЎҢгҒЈгҒҹзөҗжһңгҖҒDaSгҒҜгғЎгғғгӮ·гғҘгҒӢгӮүеӢ•з”»з”ҹжҲҗгҖҒгӮ«гғЎгғ©еҲ¶еҫЎгҖҒгғўгғјгӮ·гғ§гғіи»ўйҖҒгҖҒгӮӘгғ–гӮёгӮ§гӮҜгғҲж“ҚдҪңгҒӘгҒ©гҒ®еӨҡж§ҳгҒӘгӮҝгӮ№гӮҜгҒ«гҒҠгҒ„гҒҰеј·еҠӣгҒӘеҲ¶еҫЎиғҪеҠӣгӮ’зӨәгҒ—гҒҫгҒ—гҒҹгҖӮ # Summary (by gpt-4o-mini) - ж–°гҒ—гҒ„гӮўгғ—гғӯгғјгғҒгҖҢDiffusion as ShaderпјҲDaSпјүгҖҚгӮ’жҸҗжЎҲгҒ—гҖҒ3DеҲ¶еҫЎдҝЎеҸ·гӮ’жҙ»з”ЁгҒ—гҒҰеӢ•з”»з”ҹжҲҗгҒ®еӨҡж§ҳгҒӘеҲ¶еҫЎгӮ’е®ҹзҸҫгҖӮеҫ“жқҘгҒ®2DеҲ¶еҫЎдҝЎеҸ·гҒ«еҜҫгҒ—гҖҒ3DгғҲгғ©гғғгӮӯгғігӮ°еӢ•з”»гӮ’з”ЁгҒ„гӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгҒ§гҖҒжҷӮй–“зҡ„дёҖиІ«жҖ§гҒҢеҗ‘дёҠгҒ—гҖҒе№…еәғгҒ„еӢ•з”»еҲ¶еҫЎгӮҝгӮ№гӮҜгҒ«еј·еҠӣгҒӘжҖ§иғҪгӮ’зҷәжҸ®гҖӮ

paper-entry.title: [Paper Note] LongVie 2: Multimodal Controllable Ultra-Long Video World Model, Jianxiong Gao+, arXiv'25, 2025.12
paper-entry.tags: ComputerVision, MultiModal, DiffusionModel, LongSequence, VideoGeneration/Understandings, WorldModels, 4D (Video), reading, One-Line Notes, DepthEstimation
paper-entry.issue-body: # URL - https://arxiv.org/abs/2512.13604 # Authors - Jianxiong Gao - Zhaoxi Chen - Xian Liu - Junhao Zhuang - Chengming Xu - Jianfeng Feng - Yu Qiao - Yanwei Fu - Chenyang Si - Ziwei Liu # Abstract - Building video world models upon pretrained video generation systems represents an important yet challenging step toward general spatiotemporal intelligence. A world model should possess three essential properties: controllability, long-term visual quality, and temporal consistency. To this end, we take a progressive approach-first enhancing controllability and then extending toward long-term, high-quality generation. We present LongVie 2, an end-to-end autoregressive framework trained in three stages: (1) Multi-modal guidance, which integrates dense and sparse control signals to provide implicit world-level supervision and improve controllability; (2) Degradation-aware training on the input frame, bridging the gap between training and long-term inference to maintain high visual quality; and (3) History-context guidance, which aligns contextual information across adjacent clips to ensure temporal consistency. We further introduce LongVGenBench, a comprehensive benchmark comprising 100 high-resolution one-minute videos covering diverse real-world and synthetic environments. Extensive experiments demonstrate that LongVie 2 achieves state-of-the-art performance in long-range controllability, temporal coherence, and visual fidelity, and supports continuous video generation lasting up to five minutes, marking a significant step toward unified video world modeling. # Translation (by gpt-4o-mini) - жңҖеӨ§5еҲҶй–“гҒ®long videoгҒ®з”ҹжҲҗгҒҢеҸҜиғҪгҒ§гҖҒгғһгғ«гғҒгғўгғјгғҖгғ«гҒӘе…ҘеҠӣпјҲdepth mapпјҲз©әй–“гҒ®ж§ӢйҖ гҒ®еҲ¶еҫЎ; dense control signalпјү, point map(гӮӯгғјгғқгӮӨгғігғҲгҒ®жҷӮй–“и»ёгҒ§гҒ®и»Ңи·Ў; sparse control signalпјү)гҒ«еҝңгҒҳгҒҰз”ҹжҲҗгӮ’гӮігғігғҲгғӯгғјгғ«гҒ—гҖҒtemporal consistencyгӮӮеҗ‘дёҠгҒ—гҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгҒЁгҒ®гҒ“гҒЁгҖӮ

paper-entry.title: [Paper Note] Fast and Accurate Causal Parallel Decoding using Jacobi Forcing, Lanxiang Hu+, arXiv'25, 2025.12
paper-entry.tags: EfficiencyImprovement, NLP, LanguageModel, DiffusionModel, Decoding, read-later, Selected Papers/Blogs
paper-entry.issue-body: # URL - https://arxiv.org/abs/2512.14681 # Authors - Lanxiang Hu - Siqi Kou - Yichao Fu - Samyam Rajbhandari - Tajana Rosing - Yuxiong He - Zhijie Deng - Hao Zhang # Abstract - Multi-token generation has emerged as a promising paradigm for accelerating transformer-based large model inference. Recent efforts primarily explore diffusion Large Language Models (dLLMs) for parallel decoding to reduce inference latency. To achieve AR-level generation quality, many techniques adapt AR models into dLLMs to enable parallel decoding. However, they suffer from limited speedup compared to AR models due to a pretrain-to-posttrain mismatch. Specifically, the masked data distribution in post-training deviates significantly from the real-world data distribution seen during pretraining, and dLLMs rely on bidirectional attention, which conflicts with the causal prior learned during pretraining and hinders the integration of exact KV cache reuse. To address this, we introduce Jacobi Forcing, a progressive distillation paradigm where models are trained on their own generated parallel decoding trajectories, smoothly shifting AR models into efficient parallel decoders while preserving their pretrained causal inference property. The models trained under this paradigm, Jacobi Forcing Model, achieves 3.8x wall-clock speedup on coding and math benchmarks with minimal loss in performance. Based on Jacobi Forcing Models' trajectory characteristics, we introduce multi-block decoding with rejection recycling, which enables up to 4.5x higher token acceptance count per iteration and nearly 4.0x wall-clock speedup, effectively trading additional compute for lower inference latency. Our code is available at https://github.com/hao-ai-lab/JacobiForcing. # Translation (by gpt-4o-mini) - гғһгғ«гғҒгғҲгғјгӮҜгғіз”ҹжҲҗгҒҜгҖҒгғҲгғ©гғігӮ№гғ•гӮ©гғјгғһгғјгғҷгғјгӮ№гҒ®еӨ§иҰҸжЁЎгғўгғҮгғ«жҺЁи«–гӮ’еҠ йҖҹгҒҷгӮӢгҒҹгӮҒгҒ®жңүжңӣгҒӘгғ‘гғ©гғҖгӮӨгғ гҒЁгҒ—гҒҰжө®дёҠгҒ—гҒҰгҒ„гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮжңҖиҝ‘гҒ®еҸ–гӮҠзө„гҒҝгҒҜгҖҒдё»гҒ«жҺЁи«–гҒ®гғ¬гӮӨгғҶгғігӮ·гӮ’еүҠжёӣгҒҷгӮӢгҒҹгӮҒгҒ«гҖҒдёҰеҲ—гғҮгӮігғјгғҮгӮЈгғігӮ°гҒ®гҒҹгӮҒгҒ®жӢЎж•ЈеһӢеӨ§иҰҸжЁЎиЁҖиӘһгғўгғҮгғ«пјҲdLLMsпјүгӮ’жҺўжұӮгҒ—гҒҰгҒ„гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮARгғ¬гғҷгғ«гҒ®з”ҹжҲҗе“ҒиіӘгӮ’йҒ”жҲҗгҒҷгӮӢгҒҹгӮҒгҒ«гҖҒеӨҡгҒҸгҒ®жҠҖиЎ“гҒҢARгғўгғҮгғ«гӮ’dLLMsгҒ«йҒ©еҝңгҒ•гҒӣгҖҒдёҰеҲ—гғҮгӮігғјгғҮгӮЈгғігӮ°гӮ’еҸҜиғҪгҒ«гҒ—гҒҰгҒ„гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮгҒ—гҒӢгҒ—гҖҒдәӢеүҚеӯҰзҝ’гҒЁдәӢеҫҢеӯҰзҝ’гҒ®дёҚдёҖиҮҙгҒ®гҒҹгӮҒгҖҒARгғўгғҮгғ«гҒ«жҜ”гҒ№гҒҰйҷҗгӮүгӮҢгҒҹгӮ№гғ”гғјгғүгӮўгғғгғ—гҒ«жӮ©гҒҫгҒ•гӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮе…·дҪ“зҡ„гҒ«гҒҜгҖҒдәӢеҫҢеӯҰзҝ’гҒ«гҒҠгҒ‘гӮӢгғһгӮ№гӮҜгҒ•гӮҢгҒҹгғҮгғјгӮҝеҲҶеёғгҒҜгҖҒдәӢеүҚеӯҰзҝ’дёӯгҒ«иҰӢгӮүгӮҢгҒҹе®ҹдё–з•ҢгҒ®гғҮгғјгӮҝеҲҶеёғгҒӢгӮүеӨ§гҒҚгҒҸйҖёи„ұгҒ—гҒҰгҒҠгӮҠгҖҒdLLMsгҒҜеҸҢж–№еҗ‘гҒ®жіЁж„ҸгҒ«дҫқеӯҳгҒ—гҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгҒҹгӮҒгҖҒдәӢеүҚеӯҰзҝ’дёӯгҒ«еӯҰзҝ’гҒ•гӮҢгҒҹеӣ жһңзҡ„е…ҲиЎҢжғ…е ұгҒЁзҹӣзӣҫгҒ—гҖҒжӯЈзўәгҒӘKVгӮӯгғЈгғғгӮ·гғҘгҒ®еҶҚеҲ©з”ЁгҒ®зөұеҗҲгӮ’еҰЁгҒ’гҒҰгҒ„гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮгҒ“гӮҢгҒ«еҜҫеҮҰгҒҷгӮӢгҒҹгӮҒгҒ«гҖҒжҲ‘гҖ…гҒҜJacobi ForcingгӮ’е°Һе…ҘгҒ—гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮгҒ“гӮҢгҒҜгҖҒгғўгғҮгғ«гҒҢиҮӘгӮүз”ҹжҲҗгҒ—гҒҹдёҰеҲ—гғҮгӮігғјгғҮгӮЈгғігӮ°гҒ®и»Ңи·ЎгҒ§иЁ“з·ҙгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢйҖІиЎҢзҡ„и’ёз•ҷгғ‘гғ©гғҖгӮӨгғ гҒ§гҒӮгӮҠгҖҒARгғўгғҮгғ«гӮ’еҠ№зҺҮзҡ„гҒӘдёҰеҲ—гғҮгӮігғјгғҖгғјгҒ«гӮ№гғ гғјгӮәгҒ«з§»иЎҢгҒ•гҒӣгҒӨгҒӨгҖҒдәӢеүҚеӯҰзҝ’гҒ•гӮҢгҒҹеӣ жһңжҺЁи«–гҒ®зү№жҖ§гӮ’дҝқжҢҒгҒ—гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮгҒ“гҒ®гғ‘гғ©гғҖгӮӨгғ гҒ®дёӢгҒ§иЁ“з·ҙгҒ•гӮҢгҒҹгғўгғҮгғ«гҖҒJacobi Forcing ModelгҒҜгҖҒгӮігғјгғҮгӮЈгғігӮ°гҒҠгӮҲгҒіж•°еӯҰгҒ®гғҷгғігғҒгғһгғјгӮҜгҒ§3.8еҖҚгҒ®гӮҰгӮ©гғјгғ«гӮҜгғӯгғғгӮҜйҖҹеәҰеҗ‘дёҠгӮ’йҒ”жҲҗгҒ—гҖҒжҖ§иғҪгҒ®жҗҚеӨұгӮ’жңҖе°ҸйҷҗгҒ«жҠ‘гҒҲгҒҰгҒ„гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮJacobi Forcing ModelsгҒ®и»Ңи·Ўзү№жҖ§гҒ«еҹәгҒҘгҒҚгҖҒжӢ’еҗҰгғӘгӮөгӮӨгӮҜгғӘгғігӮ°гӮ’дјҙгҒҶгғһгғ«гғҒгғ–гғӯгғғгӮҜгғҮгӮігғјгғҮгӮЈгғігӮ°гӮ’е°Һе…ҘгҒ—гҖҒеҗ„гӮӨгғҶгғ¬гғјгӮ·гғ§гғігҒ§жңҖеӨ§4.5еҖҚгҒ®гғҲгғјгӮҜгғіеҸ—гҒ‘е…ҘгӮҢж•°гӮ’е®ҹзҸҫгҒ—гҖҒгҒ»гҒј4.0еҖҚгҒ®гӮҰгӮ©гғјгғ«гӮҜгғӯгғғгӮҜйҖҹеәҰеҗ‘дёҠгӮ’йҒ”жҲҗгҒ—гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮгҒ“гӮҢгҒ«гӮҲгӮҠгҖҒиҝҪеҠ гҒ®иЁҲз®—гӮ’гғҲгғ¬гғјгғүгӮӘгғ•гҒЁгҒ—гҒҰгҖҒжҺЁи«–гҒ®гғ¬гӮӨгғҶгғігӮ·гӮ’дҪҺдёӢгҒ•гҒӣгӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгҒҢеҸҜиғҪгҒ«гҒӘгӮҠгҒҫгҒҷгҖӮз§ҒгҒҹгҒЎгҒ®гӮігғјгғүгҒҜгҖҒhttps://github.com/hao-ai-lab/JacobiForcing гҒ§е…ҘжүӢеҸҜиғҪгҒ§гҒҷгҖӮ # Summary (by gpt-4o-mini) - гғһгғ«гғҒгғҲгғјгӮҜгғіз”ҹжҲҗгҒ«гҒҠгҒ„гҒҰгҖҒJacobi ForcingгӮ’е°Һе…ҘгҒ—гҖҒARгғўгғҮгғ«гҒӢгӮүеҠ№зҺҮзҡ„гҒӘдёҰеҲ—гғҮгӮігғјгғҖгғјгҒёгҒ®з§»иЎҢгӮ’е®ҹзҸҫгҖӮгҒ“гӮҢгҒ«гӮҲгӮҠгҖҒгӮігғјгғҮгӮЈгғігӮ°гҒЁж•°еӯҰгҒ®гғҷгғігғҒгғһгғјгӮҜгҒ§3.8еҖҚгҒ®йҖҹеәҰеҗ‘дёҠгӮ’йҒ”жҲҗгҒ—гҖҒгғһгғ«гғҒгғ–гғӯгғғгӮҜгғҮгӮігғјгғҮгӮЈгғігӮ°гҒ§жңҖеӨ§4.5еҖҚгҒ®гғҲгғјгӮҜгғіеҸ—гҒ‘е…ҘгӮҢж•°гӮ’е®ҹзҸҫгҖӮжҺЁи«–гҒ®гғ¬гӮӨгғҶгғігӮ·гӮ’дҪҺдёӢгҒ•гҒӣгӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгҒҢеҸҜиғҪгҒ«гҖӮ

paper-entry.title: [Paper Note] Diffusion Transformers with Representation Autoencoders, Boyang Zheng+, arXiv'25, 2025.10
paper-entry.tags: ComputerVision, Transformer, DiffusionModel, TextToImageGeneration, Selected Papers/Blogs, 2D (Image), reading, One-Line Notes, ImageSynthesis, AutoEncoder
paper-entry.issue-body: # URL - https://arxiv.org/abs/2510.11690 # Authors - Boyang Zheng - Nanye Ma - Shengbang Tong - Saining Xie # Abstract - Latent generative modeling, where a pretrained autoencoder maps pixels into a latent space for the diffusion process, has become the standard strategy for Diffusion Transformers (DiT); however, the autoencoder component has barely evolved. Most DiTs continue to rely on the original VAE encoder, which introduces several limitations: outdated backbones that compromise architectural simplicity, low-dimensional latent spaces that restrict information capacity, and weak representations that result from purely reconstruction-based training and ultimately limit generative quality. In this work, we explore replacing the VAE with pretrained representation encoders (e.g., DINO, SigLIP, MAE) paired with trained decoders, forming what we term Representation Autoencoders (RAEs). These models provide both high-quality reconstructions and semantically rich latent spaces, while allowing for a scalable transformer-based architecture. Since these latent spaces are typically high-dimensional, a key challenge is enabling diffusion transformers to operate effectively within them. We analyze the sources of this difficulty, propose theoretically motivated solutions, and validate them empirically. Our approach achieves faster convergence without auxiliary representation alignment losses. Using a DiT variant equipped with a lightweight, wide DDT head, we achieve strong image generation results on ImageNet: 1.51 FID at 256x256 (no guidance) and 1.13 at both 256x256 and 512x512 (with guidance). RAE offers clear advantages and should be the new default for diffusion transformer training. # Translation (by gpt-4o-mini) - TransformersпјҲDiTпјүгҒ®жЁҷжә–зҡ„гҒӘжҲҰз•ҘгҒЁгҒӘгҒЈгҒҰгҒ„гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮгҒ—гҒӢгҒ—гҖҒгӮӘгғјгғҲгӮЁгғігӮігғјгғҖгҒ®гӮігғігғқгғјгғҚгғігғҲгҒҜгҒ»гҒЁгӮ“гҒ©йҖІеҢ–гҒ—гҒҰгҒ„гҒҫгҒӣгӮ“гҖӮгҒ»гҒЁгӮ“гҒ©гҒ®DiTгҒҜгҖҒе…ғгҒ®VAEгӮЁгғігӮігғјгғҖгҒ«дҫқеӯҳгҒ—гҒҰгҒҠгӮҠгҖҒгҒ“гӮҢгҒ«гҒҜгҒ„гҒҸгҒӨгҒӢгҒ®еҲ¶йҷҗгҒҢгҒӮгӮҠгҒҫгҒҷгҖӮе…·дҪ“зҡ„гҒ«гҒҜгҖҒгӮўгғјгӮӯгғҶгӮҜгғҒгғЈгҒ®гӮ·гғігғ—гғ«гҒ•гӮ’жҗҚгҒӘгҒҶеҸӨгҒ„гғҗгғғгӮҜгғңгғјгғігҖҒжғ…е ұе®№йҮҸгӮ’еҲ¶йҷҗгҒҷгӮӢдҪҺж¬Ўе…ғгҒ®жҪңеңЁз©әй–“гҖҒзҙ”зІӢгҒ«еҶҚж§ӢжҲҗгғҷгғјгӮ№гҒ®гғҲгғ¬гғјгғӢгғігӮ°гҒӢгӮүз”ҹгҒҳгӮӢејұгҒ„иЎЁзҸҫгҒҢгҒӮгӮҠгҖҒжңҖзөӮзҡ„гҒ«гҒҜз”ҹжҲҗе“ҒиіӘгӮ’еҲ¶йҷҗгҒ—гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮжң¬з ”з©¶гҒ§гҒҜгҖҒVAEгӮ’дәӢеүҚеӯҰзҝ’гҒ•гӮҢгҒҹиЎЁзҸҫгӮЁгғігӮігғјгғҖпјҲдҫӢпјҡDINOгҖҒSigLIPгҖҒMAEпјүгҒЁгғҲгғ¬гғјгғӢгғігӮ°гҒ•гӮҢгҒҹгғҮгӮігғјгғҖгҒ«зҪ®гҒҚжҸӣгҒҲгӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгӮ’жҺўжұӮгҒ—гҖҒгҒ“гӮҢгӮ’иЎЁзҸҫгӮӘгғјгғҲгӮЁгғігӮігғјгғҖпјҲRAEпјүгҒЁе‘јгҒігҒҫгҒҷгҖӮгҒ“гӮҢгӮүгҒ®гғўгғҮгғ«гҒҜгҖҒй«ҳе“ҒиіӘгҒӘеҶҚж§ӢжҲҗгҒЁж„Ҹе‘ізҡ„гҒ«иұҠгҒӢгҒӘжҪңеңЁз©әй–“гӮ’жҸҗдҫӣгҒ—гҖҒгӮ№гӮұгғјгғ©гғ–гғ«гҒӘгғҲгғ©гғігӮ№гғ•гӮ©гғјгғһгғјгғҷгғјгӮ№гҒ®гӮўгғјгӮӯгғҶгӮҜгғҒгғЈгӮ’еҸҜиғҪгҒ«гҒ—гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮгҒ“гӮҢгӮүгҒ®жҪңеңЁз©әй–“гҒҜйҖҡеёёй«ҳж¬Ўе…ғгҒ§гҒӮгӮӢгҒҹгӮҒгҖҒжӢЎж•ЈгғҲгғ©гғігӮ№гғ•гӮ©гғјгғһгғјгҒҢгҒқгӮҢгӮүгҒ®дёӯгҒ§еҠ№жһңзҡ„гҒ«ж©ҹиғҪгҒҷгӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгӮ’еҸҜиғҪгҒ«гҒҷгӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгҒҢйҮҚиҰҒгҒӘиӘІйЎҢгҒ§гҒҷгҖӮжҲ‘гҖ…гҒҜгҒ“гҒ®еӣ°йӣЈгҒ®еҺҹеӣ гӮ’еҲҶжһҗгҒ—гҖҒзҗҶи«–зҡ„гҒ«ж №жӢ гҒ®гҒӮгӮӢи§Јжұәзӯ–гӮ’жҸҗжЎҲгҒ—гҖҒе®ҹиЁјзҡ„гҒ«жӨңиЁјгҒ—гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮжҲ‘гҖ…гҒ®гӮўгғ—гғӯгғјгғҒгҒҜгҖҒиЈңеҠ©зҡ„гҒӘиЎЁзҸҫж•ҙеҗҲжҗҚеӨұгҒӘгҒ—гҒ§гӮҲгӮҠж—©гҒ„еҸҺжқҹгӮ’йҒ”жҲҗгҒ—гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮи»ҪйҮҸгҒ§еәғгҒ„DDTгғҳгғғгғүгӮ’иЈ…еӮҷгҒ—гҒҹDiTгҒ®гғҗгғӘгӮўгғігғҲгӮ’дҪҝз”ЁгҒ—гҒҰгҖҒImageNetгҒ§еј·еҠӣгҒӘз”»еғҸз”ҹжҲҗзөҗжһңгӮ’йҒ”жҲҗгҒ—гҒҫгҒ—гҒҹпјҡ256x256гҒ§1.51 FIDпјҲгӮ¬гӮӨгғҖгғігӮ№гҒӘгҒ—пјүгҖҒ256x256гҒҠгӮҲгҒі512x512гҒ§1.13пјҲгӮ¬гӮӨгғҖгғігӮ№гҒӮгӮҠпјүгҖӮRAEгҒҜжҳҺзўәгҒӘеҲ©зӮ№гӮ’жҸҗдҫӣгҒ—гҖҒжӢЎж•ЈгғҲгғ©гғігӮ№гғ•гӮ©гғјгғһгғјгҒ®гғҲгғ¬гғјгғӢгғігӮ°гҒ«гҒҠгҒ‘гӮӢж–°гҒ—гҒ„гғҮгғ•гӮ©гғ«гғҲгҒЁгҒӘгӮӢгҒ№гҒҚгҒ§гҒҷгҖӮ paper-entry.title: [Paper Note] Latent Diffusion Model without Variational Autoencoder, Minglei Shi+, arXiv'25, 2025.10
paper-entry.tags: ComputerVision, DiffusionModel, Selected Papers/Blogs, Encoder-Decoder, Backbone, KeyPoint Notes, ImageSynthesis
paper-entry.issue-body: # URL - https://arxiv.org/abs/2510.15301 # Authors - Minglei Shi - Haolin Wang - Wenzhao Zheng - Ziyang Yuan - Xiaoshi Wu - Xintao Wang - Pengfei Wan - Jie Zhou - Jiwen Lu # Abstract - Recent progress in diffusion-based visual generation has largely relied on latent diffusion models with variational autoencoders (VAEs). While effective for high-fidelity synthesis, this VAE+diffusion paradigm suffers from limited training efficiency, slow inference, and poor transferability to broader vision tasks. These issues stem from a key limitation of VAE latent spaces: the lack of clear semantic separation and strong discriminative structure. Our analysis confirms that these properties are crucial not only for perception and understanding tasks, but also for the stable and efficient training of latent diffusion models. Motivated by this insight, we introduce SVG, a novel latent diffusion model without variational autoencoders, which leverages self-supervised representations for visual generation. SVG constructs a feature space with clear semantic discriminability by leveraging frozen DINO features, while a lightweight residual branch captures fine-grained details for high-fidelity reconstruction. Diffusion models are trained directly on this semantically structured latent space to facilitate more efficient learning. As a result, SVG enables accelerated diffusion training, supports few-step sampling, and improves generative quality. Experimental results further show that SVG preserves the semantic and discriminative capabilities of the underlying self-supervised representations, providing a principled pathway toward task-general, high-quality visual representations. Code and interpretations are available at https://howlin-wang.github.io/svg/. # Translation (by gpt-4o-mini) - жңҖиҝ‘гҒ®жӢЎж•ЈгғҷгғјгӮ№гҒ®иҰ–иҰҡз”ҹжҲҗгҒ«гҒҠгҒ‘гӮӢйҖІеұ•гҒҜгҖҒдё»гҒ«еӨүеҲҶгӮӘгғјгғҲгӮЁгғігӮігғјгғҖпјҲVAEпјүгӮ’з”ЁгҒ„гҒҹжҪңеңЁжӢЎж•ЈгғўгғҮгғ«гҒ«дҫқеӯҳгҒ—гҒҰгҒ„гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮгҒ“гҒ®VAE+жӢЎж•ЈгҒ®гғ‘гғ©гғҖгӮӨгғ гҒҜгҖҒй«ҳеҝ е®ҹеәҰгҒ®еҗҲжҲҗгҒ«гҒҜеҠ№жһңзҡ„гҒ§гҒҷгҒҢгҖҒйҷҗгӮүгӮҢгҒҹгғҲгғ¬гғјгғӢгғігӮ°еҠ№зҺҮгҖҒйҒ…гҒ„жҺЁи«–гҖҒгҒқгҒ—гҒҰеәғзҜ„гҒӘиҰ–иҰҡгӮҝгӮ№гӮҜгҒёгҒ®з§»иЎҢжҖ§гҒ®дҪҺгҒ•гҒЁгҒ„гҒҶе•ҸйЎҢгӮ’жҠұгҒҲгҒҰгҒ„гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮгҒ“гӮҢгӮүгҒ®е•ҸйЎҢгҒҜгҖҒVAEгҒ®жҪңеңЁз©әй–“гҒ®йҮҚиҰҒгҒӘеҲ¶йҷҗгҒ«иө·еӣ гҒ—гҒҰгҒ„гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮгҒқгӮҢгҒҜгҖҒжҳҺзўәгҒӘж„Ҹе‘ізҡ„еҲҶйӣўгҒЁеј·еҠӣгҒӘиӯҳеҲҘж§ӢйҖ гҒ®ж¬ еҰӮгҒ§гҒҷгҖӮжҲ‘гҖ…гҒ®еҲҶжһҗгҒҜгҖҒгҒ“гӮҢгӮүгҒ®зү№жҖ§гҒҢзҹҘиҰҡгӮ„зҗҶи§ЈгӮҝгӮ№гӮҜгҒ гҒ‘гҒ§гҒӘгҒҸгҖҒжҪңеңЁжӢЎж•ЈгғўгғҮгғ«гҒ®е®үе®ҡгҒӢгҒӨеҠ№зҺҮзҡ„гҒӘгғҲгғ¬гғјгғӢгғігӮ°гҒ«гӮӮйҮҚиҰҒгҒ§гҒӮгӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгӮ’зўәиӘҚгҒ—гҒҫгҒ—гҒҹгҖӮгҒ“гҒ®жҙһеҜҹгҒ«еҹәгҒҘгҒҚгҖҒжҲ‘гҖ…гҒҜеӨүеҲҶгӮӘгғјгғҲгӮЁгғігӮігғјгғҖгӮ’дҪҝз”ЁгҒ—гҒӘгҒ„ж–°гҒ—гҒ„жҪңеңЁжӢЎж•ЈгғўгғҮгғ«SVGгӮ’жҸҗжЎҲгҒ—гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮSVGгҒҜгҖҒиҰ–иҰҡз”ҹжҲҗгҒ®гҒҹгӮҒгҒ«иҮӘе·ұж•ҷеё«гҒӮгӮҠиЎЁзҸҫгӮ’жҙ»з”ЁгҒ—гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮSVGгҒҜгҖҒеҮҚзөҗгҒ•гӮҢгҒҹDINOзү№еҫҙгӮ’еҲ©з”ЁгҒ—гҒҰжҳҺзўәгҒӘж„Ҹе‘ізҡ„иӯҳеҲҘжҖ§гӮ’жҢҒгҒӨзү№еҫҙз©әй–“гӮ’ж§ӢзҜүгҒ—гҖҒи»ҪйҮҸгҒӘж®Ӣе·®гғ–гғ©гғігғҒгҒҢй«ҳеҝ е®ҹеәҰгҒ®еҶҚж§ӢжҲҗгҒ®гҒҹгӮҒгҒ®зҙ°гҒӢгҒ„и©ізҙ°гӮ’жҚүгҒҲгҒҫгҒҷгҖӮжӢЎж•ЈгғўгғҮгғ«гҒҜгҖҒгҒ“гҒ®ж„Ҹе‘ізҡ„гҒ«ж§ӢйҖ еҢ–гҒ•гӮҢгҒҹжҪңеңЁз©әй–“дёҠгҒ§зӣҙжҺҘгғҲгғ¬гғјгғӢгғігӮ°гҒ•гӮҢгҖҒгӮҲгӮҠеҠ№зҺҮзҡ„гҒӘеӯҰзҝ’гӮ’дҝғйҖІгҒ—гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮгҒқгҒ®зөҗжһңгҖҒSVGгҒҜжӢЎж•ЈгғҲгғ¬гғјгғӢгғігӮ°гӮ’еҠ йҖҹгҒ—гҖҒе°‘гҒӘгҒ„гӮ№гғҶгғғгғ—гҒ§гҒ®гӮөгғігғ—гғӘгғігӮ°гӮ’гӮөгғқгғјгғҲгҒ—гҖҒз”ҹжҲҗе“ҒиіӘгӮ’еҗ‘дёҠгҒ•гҒӣгҒҫгҒҷгҖӮе®ҹйЁ“зөҗжһңгҒҜгҒ•гӮүгҒ«гҖҒSVGгҒҢеҹәзӣӨгҒЁгҒӘгӮӢиҮӘе·ұж•ҷеё«гҒӮгӮҠиЎЁзҸҫгҒ®ж„Ҹе‘ізҡ„гҒҠгӮҲгҒіиӯҳеҲҘиғҪеҠӣгӮ’дҝқжҢҒгҒ—гҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгӮ’зӨәгҒ—гҒҰгҒҠгӮҠгҖҒгӮҝгӮ№гӮҜдёҖиҲ¬зҡ„гҒ§й«ҳе“ҒиіӘгҒӘиҰ–иҰҡиЎЁзҸҫгҒёгҒ®еҺҹеүҮзҡ„гҒӘйҒ“зӯӢгӮ’жҸҗдҫӣгҒ—гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮгӮігғјгғүгҒЁи§ЈйҮҲгҒҜгҖҒhttps://howlin-wang.github.io/svg/ гҒ§е…ҘжүӢеҸҜиғҪгҒ§гҒҷгҖӮ # Summary (by gpt-4o-mini) - VAEгӮ’з”ЁгҒ„гҒӘгҒ„ж–°гҒ—гҒ„жҪңеңЁжӢЎж•ЈгғўгғҮгғ«SVGгӮ’жҸҗжЎҲгҖӮSVGгҒҜиҮӘе·ұж•ҷеё«гҒӮгӮҠиЎЁзҸҫгӮ’жҙ»з”ЁгҒ—гҖҒжҳҺзўәгҒӘж„Ҹе‘ізҡ„иӯҳеҲҘжҖ§гӮ’жҢҒгҒӨзү№еҫҙз©әй–“гӮ’ж§ӢзҜүгҖӮгҒ“гӮҢгҒ«гӮҲгӮҠгҖҒжӢЎж•ЈгғҲгғ¬гғјгғӢгғігӮ°гҒҢеҠ йҖҹгҒ—гҖҒз”ҹжҲҗе“ҒиіӘгҒҢеҗ‘дёҠгҖӮе®ҹйЁ“зөҗжһңгҒҜSVGгҒ®й«ҳе“ҒиіӘгҒӘиҰ–иҰҡиЎЁзҸҫиғҪеҠӣгӮ’зӨәгҒҷгҖӮ

maintainer-comment-text.p: гҒ“гӮҢгҒҫгҒ§гҒ®жӢЎж•ЈгғўгғҮгғ«гғҷгғјгӮ№гҒ®Image GeneiationгғўгғҮгғ«гҒ«гҒҠгҒ‘гӮӢVAEгӮ’гҖҒдәӢеүҚеӯҰзҝ’жёҲгҒҝпјҲself supervised learning)гҒ®vision encoderпјҲжң¬зЁҝгҒ§гҒҜDINOv3)гҒ«зҪ®гҒҚжҸӣгҒҲfreezeгҒ—гҖҒгҒқгӮҢгҒЁгҒҜеҲҘйҖ”Residual EncoderгҒЁе‘јгҒ°гӮҢгӮӢViTгғҷгғјгӮ№гҒ®EncoderгӮ’еӯҰзҝ’гҒҷгӮӢгҖӮеүҚиҖ…гҒҜз”»еғҸгҒ®ж„Ҹе‘іжғ…е ұгӮ’жҚүгҒҲгӮӢиғҪеҠӣгӮ’гҒқгҒ®гҒҫгҒҫдҝқжҢҒгҒ—гҖҒResidual EncoderеҒҙгҒ§ReconstructionгӮ’гҒҷгӮӢдёҠгҒ§гҒ®PerceptualгҒӘжғ…е ұзӯүгҒ®пјҲvision encoderгҒ§гҒҜеӨұгӮҸгӮҢгҒҰгҒ—гҒҫгҒҶпјүгӮҲгӮҠзІҫз·»гҒӘзү№еҫҙгӮ’жҚүгҒҲгӮӢгҖӮеҸҢж–№гҒ®EncoderеҮәеҠӣгҒҜchannelж¬Ўе…ғгҒ§concatгҒ•гӮҢгҖҒSVG FeatureгӮ’еҪўжҲҗгҒҷгӮӢгҖӮSVG DecoderгҒҜгҖҒSVG FeatureгӮ’PixelгӮ№гғҡгғјгӮ№гҒ«жҲ»гҒҷеҪ№еүІгӮ’жһңгҒҹгҒҷгҖӮгҒ“гҒ®гӮўгғјгӮӯгғҶгӮҜгғҒгғЈгҒҜгӮ·гғігғ—гғ«гҒ§и»ҪйҮҸгҒ гҒҢгҖҒDINOv3гҒ«гӮҲгӮӢеј·еҠӣгҒӘж„Ҹе‘ізҡ„гҒӘиӯҳеҲҘеҠӣгӮ’дҝқгҒЎгҒӨгҒӨгҖҒзІҫз·»гҒӘзү№еҫҙгӮ’жҚүгҒҲгӮӢиғҪеҠӣгӮ’иЈңе®ҢгҒ§гҒҚгӮӢгҖӮFigure 5гӮ’иҰӢгӮӢгҒЁгҖҒе®ҹйҡӣгҒ«DINOv3гҒ®гҒҝгҒЁжҜ”ијғгҒ—гҒҰгҖҒResidual EncoderгҒ«гӮҲгҒЈгҒҰгҖҒзҙ°гҒӢгҒ„йғЁеҲҶгҒҢгӮҲгӮҠжӯЈзўәгҒӘReconstructionгҒҢе®ҹзҸҫгҒ§гҒҚгҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгҒҢе®ҡжҖ§зҡ„гҒ«гӮҸгҒӢгӮӢгҖӮеӯҰзҝ’жҷӮгҒҜReconstruction lossгӮ’дҪҝгҒҶгҒҢгҖҒResidual EncoderгҒ«йҒҺеү°гҒ«дҫқеӯҳгҒҷгӮӢгҒ гҒ‘гӮҒгҒӘгҒҸгҖҒoutputгҒ®ж•°еҖӨзҡ„гҒӘеҖӨеҹҹгҒҢз•°гҒӘгӮҠгҖҒDINOv3гҒ®ж„Ҹе‘іжғ…е ұгӮ’жҗҚгҒӘгҒҶжҒҗгӮҢгҒҢи¶ігӮӢгҒҹгӮҒгҖҒResidual EncoderгҒ®еҮәеҠӣгҒ®еҲҶеёғгӮ’DINOv3гҒЁalignгҒҷгӮӢгӮҲгҒҶгҒ«еӯҰзҝ’гҒҷгӮӢгҖӮ VAE EncoderгҒ«гӮҲгӮӢlatent vectorгҒҜдҪҺж¬Ўе…ғгҒ гҒҢгҖҒжҸҗжЎҲжүӢжі•гҒҜгӮҲгӮҠй«ҳж¬Ўе…ғгҒӘгғҷгӮҜгғҲгғ«гӮ’жүұгҒҶгҒҹгӮҒгҖҒDiffusionгғўгғҮгғ«гҒ®еӯҰзҝ’гҒҢйӣЈгҒ—гҒ„гҒЁиҖғгҒҲгӮүгӮҢгӮӢгҒҢгҖҒSVG FeatureгҒ®зү№еҫҙйҮҸгҒҜгҒҶгҒҫгҒҸеҲҶж•ЈгҒ—гҒҰгҒҠгӮҠгҖҒе®үе®ҡгҒ—гҒҰFlow MatchingгҒ§еӯҰзҝ’гҒҢгҒ§гҒҚгӮӢгҒЁгҒ®гҒ“гҒЁгҖӮ е®ҹйҡӣгҖҒе®ҹйЁ“зөҗжһңгӮ’иҰӢгӮӢгҒЁе®үе®ҡгҒ—гҒҰгҖҒгҒ—гҒӢгӮӮгӮөгғігғ—гғ«еҠ№зҺҮгҒҢгғҷгғјгӮ№гғ©гӮӨгғігҒЁжҜ”ијғгҒ—гҒҰеӨ§е№…гҒ«й«ҳгҒҸеҸҺжқҹгҒ—гҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгҒҢиҰӢеҸ—гҒ‘гӮүгӮҢгӮӢгҖӮ

paper-entry.title: [Paper Note] SVG-T2I: Scaling Up Text-to-Image Latent Diffusion Model Without Variational Autoencoder, Minglei Shi+, arXiv'25, 2025.12
paper-entry.tags: ComputerVision, DiffusionModel, TextToImageGeneration, Self-SupervisedLearning, FlowMatching, reading
paper-entry.issue-body: # URL - https://arxiv.org/abs/2512.11749 # Authors - Minglei Shi - Haolin Wang - Borui Zhang - Wenzhao Zheng - Bohan Zeng - Ziyang Yuan - Xiaoshi Wu - Yuanxing Zhang - Huan Yang - Xintao Wang - Pengfei Wan - Kun Gai - Jie Zhou - Jiwen Lu # Abstract - Visual generation grounded in Visual Foundation Model (VFM) representations offers a highly promising unified pathway for integrating visual understanding, perception, and generation. Despite this potential, training large-scale text-to-image diffusion models entirely within the VFM representation space remains largely unexplored. To bridge this gap, we scale the SVG (Self-supervised representations for Visual Generation) framework, proposing SVG-T2I to support high-quality text-to-image synthesis directly in the VFM feature domain. By leveraging a standard text-to-image diffusion pipeline, SVG-T2I achieves competitive performance, reaching 0.75 on GenEval and 85.78 on DPG-Bench. This performance validates the intrinsic representational power of VFMs for generative tasks. We fully open-source the project, including the autoencoder and generation model, together with their training, inference, evaluation pipelines, and pre-trained weights, to facilitate further research in representation-driven visual generation. # Translation (by gpt-4o-mini) - иҰ–иҰҡз”ҹжҲҗгҒҜгҖҒиҰ–иҰҡеҹәзӣӨгғўгғҮгғ«пјҲVFMпјүиЎЁзҸҫгҒ«еҹәгҒҘгҒҸгҒ“гҒЁгҒ§гҖҒиҰ–иҰҡзҗҶи§ЈгҖҒзҹҘиҰҡгҖҒз”ҹжҲҗгӮ’зөұеҗҲгҒҷгӮӢгҒҹгӮҒгҒ®йқһеёёгҒ«жңүжңӣгҒӘзөұдёҖзҡ„гӮўгғ—гғӯгғјгғҒгӮ’жҸҗдҫӣгҒ—гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮгҒ“гҒ®еҸҜиғҪжҖ§гҒ«гӮӮгҒӢгҒӢгӮҸгӮүгҒҡгҖҒVFMиЎЁзҸҫз©әй–“еҶ…гҒ§еӨ§иҰҸжЁЎгҒӘгғҶгӮӯгӮ№гғҲгҒӢгӮүз”»еғҸгҒёгҒ®жӢЎж•ЈгғўгғҮгғ«гӮ’е®Ңе…ЁгҒ«иЁ“з·ҙгҒҷгӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгҒҜгҖҒгҒ»гҒЁгӮ“гҒ©жҺўжұӮгҒ•гӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гҒҫгҒӣгӮ“гҖӮгҒ“гҒ®гӮ®гғЈгғғгғ—гӮ’еҹӢгӮҒгӮӢгҒҹгӮҒгҒ«гҖҒз§ҒгҒҹгҒЎгҒҜSVGпјҲиҰ–иҰҡз”ҹжҲҗгҒ®гҒҹгӮҒгҒ®иҮӘе·ұж•ҷеё«гҒӮгӮҠиЎЁзҸҫпјүгғ•гғ¬гғјгғ гғҜгғјгӮҜгӮ’жӢЎејөгҒ—гҖҒSVG-T2IгӮ’жҸҗжЎҲгҒ—гҒҰгҖҒVFMзү№еҫҙгғүгғЎгӮӨгғіеҶ…гҒ§й«ҳе“ҒиіӘгҒӘгғҶгӮӯгӮ№гғҲгҒӢгӮүз”»еғҸгҒёгҒ®еҗҲжҲҗгӮ’зӣҙжҺҘгӮөгғқгғјгғҲгҒ—гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮжЁҷжә–зҡ„гҒӘгғҶгӮӯгӮ№гғҲгҒӢгӮүз”»еғҸгҒёгҒ®жӢЎж•Јгғ‘гӮӨгғ—гғ©гӮӨгғігӮ’жҙ»з”ЁгҒҷгӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгҒ§гҖҒSVG-T2IгҒҜз«¶дәүеҠӣгҒ®гҒӮгӮӢжҖ§иғҪгӮ’йҒ”жҲҗгҒ—гҖҒGenEvalгҒ§0.75гҖҒDPG-BenchгҒ§85.78гҒ«еҲ°йҒ”гҒ—гҒҫгҒ—гҒҹгҖӮгҒ“гҒ®жҖ§иғҪгҒҜгҖҒз”ҹжҲҗгӮҝгӮ№гӮҜгҒ«гҒҠгҒ‘гӮӢVFMгҒ®жң¬иіӘзҡ„гҒӘиЎЁзҸҫеҠӣгӮ’жӨңиЁјгҒ—гҒҰгҒ„гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮз§ҒгҒҹгҒЎгҒҜгҖҒгғ—гғӯгӮёгӮ§гӮҜгғҲгӮ’е®Ңе…ЁгҒ«гӮӘгғјгғ—гғігӮҪгғјгӮ№еҢ–гҒ—гҖҒгӮӘгғјгғҲгӮЁгғігӮігғјгғҖгғјгҒЁз”ҹжҲҗгғўгғҮгғ«гҖҒгҒӘгӮүгҒігҒ«гҒқгӮҢгӮүгҒ®иЁ“з·ҙгҖҒжҺЁи«–гҖҒи©•дҫЎгғ‘гӮӨгғ—гғ©гӮӨгғігҖҒдәӢеүҚиЁ“з·ҙжёҲгҒҝгҒ®йҮҚгҒҝгӮ’еҗ«гӮҒгҖҒиЎЁзҸҫй§ҶеӢ•гҒ®иҰ–иҰҡз”ҹжҲҗгҒ«й–ўгҒҷгӮӢгҒ•гӮүгҒӘгӮӢз ”з©¶гӮ’дҝғйҖІгҒ—гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮ # Summary (by gpt-4o-mini) - иҰ–иҰҡз”ҹжҲҗгҒ®гҒҹгӮҒгҒ«SVG-T2Iгғ•гғ¬гғјгғ гғҜгғјгӮҜгӮ’жҸҗжЎҲгҒ—гҖҒVFMзү№еҫҙгғүгғЎгӮӨгғіеҶ…гҒ§й«ҳе“ҒиіӘгҒӘгғҶгӮӯгӮ№гғҲгҒӢгӮүз”»еғҸгҒёгҒ®еҗҲжҲҗгӮ’е®ҹзҸҫгҖӮжЁҷжә–зҡ„гҒӘжӢЎж•Јгғ‘гӮӨгғ—гғ©гӮӨгғігӮ’з”ЁгҒ„гҒҰз«¶дәүеҠӣгҒ®гҒӮгӮӢжҖ§иғҪгӮ’йҒ”жҲҗгҒ—гҖҒGenEvalгҒ§0.75гҖҒDPG-BenchгҒ§85.78гӮ’иЁҳйҢІгҖӮгғ—гғӯгӮёгӮ§гӮҜгғҲгҒҜгӮӘгғјгғ—гғігӮҪгғјгӮ№еҢ–гҒ•гӮҢгҖҒиҰ–иҰҡз”ҹжҲҗгҒ«й–ўгҒҷгӮӢз ”з©¶гӮ’дҝғйҖІгҖӮ

paper-entry.title: [Paper Note] One Layer Is Enough: Adapting Pretrained Visual Encoders for Image Generation, Yuan Gao+, arXiv'25, 2025.12
paper-entry.tags: ComputerVision, EfficiencyImprovement, DiffusionModel, SmallModel, Encoder, 2D (Image), AutoEncoder
paper-entry.issue-body: # URL - https://arxiv.org/abs/2512.07829 # Authors - Yuan Gao - Chen Chen - Tianrong Chen - Jiatao Gu # Abstract - Visual generative models (e.g., diffusion models) typically operate in compressed latent spaces to balance training efficiency and sample quality. In parallel, there has been growing interest in leveraging high-quality pre-trained visual representations, either by aligning them inside VAEs or directly within the generative model. However, adapting such representations remains challenging due to fundamental mismatches between understanding-oriented features and generation-friendly latent spaces. Representation encoders benefit from high-dimensional latents that capture diverse hypotheses for masked regions, whereas generative models favor low-dimensional latents that must faithfully preserve injected noise. This discrepancy has led prior work to rely on complex objectives and architectures. In this work, we propose FAE (Feature Auto-Encoder), a simple yet effective framework that adapts pre-trained visual representations into low-dimensional latents suitable for generation using as little as a single attention layer, while retaining sufficient information for both reconstruction and understanding. The key is to couple two separate deep decoders: one trained to reconstruct the original feature space, and a second that takes the reconstructed features as input for image generation. FAE is generic; it can be instantiated with a variety of self-supervised encoders (e.g., DINO, SigLIP) and plugged into two distinct generative families: diffusion models and normalizing flows. Across class-conditional and text-to-image benchmarks, FAE achieves strong performance. For example, on ImageNet 256x256, our diffusion model with CFG attains a near state-of-the-art FID of 1.29 (800 epochs) and 1.70 (80 epochs). Without CFG, FAE reaches the state-of-the-art FID of 1.48 (800 epochs) and 2.08 (80 epochs), demonstrating both high quality and fast learning. # Translation (by gpt-4o-mini) - иҰ–иҰҡз”ҹжҲҗгғўгғҮгғ«пјҲдҫӢпјҡжӢЎж•ЈгғўгғҮгғ«пјүгҒҜгҖҒгғҲгғ¬гғјгғӢгғігӮ°еҠ№зҺҮгҒЁгӮөгғігғ—гғ«е“ҒиіӘгҒ®гғҗгғ©гғігӮ№гӮ’еҸ–гӮӢгҒҹгӮҒгҒ«гҖҒең§зё®гҒ•гӮҢгҒҹжҪңеңЁз©әй–“гҒ§еӢ•дҪңгҒҷгӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгҒҢдёҖиҲ¬зҡ„гҒ§гҒҷгҖӮеҗҢжҷӮгҒ«гҖҒVAEеҶ…гҒ§гҒқгӮҢгӮүгӮ’ж•ҙеҲ—гҒ•гҒӣгӮӢгҒӢгҖҒз”ҹжҲҗгғўгғҮгғ«еҶ…гҒ§зӣҙжҺҘеҲ©з”ЁгҒҷгӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгҒ«гӮҲгҒЈгҒҰгҖҒй«ҳе“ҒиіӘгҒ®дәӢеүҚеӯҰзҝ’гҒ•гӮҢгҒҹиҰ–иҰҡиЎЁзҸҫгӮ’жҙ»з”ЁгҒҷгӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгҒёгҒ®й–ўеҝғгҒҢй«ҳгҒҫгҒЈгҒҰгҒ„гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮгҒ—гҒӢгҒ—гҖҒгҒқгҒ®гӮҲгҒҶгҒӘиЎЁзҸҫгӮ’йҒ©еҝңгҒ•гҒӣгӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгҒҜгҖҒзҗҶи§ЈжҢҮеҗ‘гҒ®зү№еҫҙгҒЁз”ҹжҲҗгҒ«йҒ©гҒ—гҒҹжҪңеңЁз©әй–“гҒЁгҒ®й–“гҒ«ж №жң¬зҡ„гҒӘдёҚдёҖиҮҙгҒҢгҒӮгӮӢгҒҹгӮҒгҖҒдҫқз„¶гҒЁгҒ—гҒҰеӣ°йӣЈгҒ§гҒҷгҖӮиЎЁзҸҫгӮЁгғігӮігғјгғҖгҒҜгҖҒгғһгӮ№гӮҜгҒ•гӮҢгҒҹй ҳеҹҹгҒ«еҜҫгҒҷгӮӢеӨҡж§ҳгҒӘд»®иӘ¬гӮ’жҚүгҒҲгӮӢй«ҳж¬Ўе…ғгҒ®жҪңеңЁгӮ’еҲ©з”ЁгҒҷгӮӢдёҖж–№гҒ§гҖҒз”ҹжҲҗгғўгғҮгғ«гҒҜжіЁе…ҘгҒ•гӮҢгҒҹгғҺгӮӨгӮәгӮ’еҝ е®ҹгҒ«дҝқжҢҒгҒ—гҒӘгҒ‘гӮҢгҒ°гҒӘгӮүгҒӘгҒ„дҪҺж¬Ўе…ғгҒ®жҪңеңЁгӮ’еҘҪгҒҝгҒҫгҒҷгҖӮгҒ“гҒ®дёҚдёҖиҮҙгҒҜгҖҒеҫ“жқҘгҒ®з ”з©¶гҒҢиӨҮйӣ‘гҒӘзӣ®зҡ„й–ўж•°гӮ„гӮўгғјгӮӯгғҶгӮҜгғҒгғЈгҒ«дҫқеӯҳгҒҷгӮӢеҺҹеӣ гҒЁгҒӘгҒЈгҒҰгҒ„гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮжң¬з ”з©¶гҒ§гҒҜгҖҒдәӢеүҚеӯҰзҝ’гҒ•гӮҢгҒҹиҰ–иҰҡиЎЁзҸҫгӮ’з”ҹжҲҗгҒ«йҒ©гҒ—гҒҹдҪҺж¬Ўе…ғгҒ®жҪңеңЁгҒ«йҒ©еҝңгҒ•гҒӣгӮӢгӮ·гғігғ—гғ«гҒ§гҒӮгӮҠгҒӘгҒҢгӮүеҠ№жһңзҡ„гҒӘгғ•гғ¬гғјгғ гғҜгғјгӮҜгҒ§гҒӮгӮӢFAEпјҲFeature Auto-EncoderпјүгӮ’жҸҗжЎҲгҒ—гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮFAEгҒҜгҖҒеҶҚж§ӢжҲҗгҒЁзҗҶи§ЈгҒ®дёЎж–№гҒ«еҚҒеҲҶгҒӘжғ…е ұгӮ’дҝқжҢҒгҒ—гҒӘгҒҢгӮүгҖҒгӮҸгҒҡгҒӢ1гҒӨгҒ®гӮўгғҶгғігӮ·гғ§гғіеұӨгӮ’дҪҝз”ЁгҒ—гҒҰе®ҹзҸҫгҒ—гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮйҮҚиҰҒгҒӘзӮ№гҒҜгҖҒе…ғгҒ®зү№еҫҙз©әй–“гӮ’еҶҚж§ӢжҲҗгҒҷгӮӢгҒҹгӮҒгҒ«иЁ“з·ҙгҒ•гӮҢгҒҹ2гҒӨгҒ®еҲҘгҖ…гҒ®ж·ұеұӨгғҮгӮігғјгғҖгӮ’зө„гҒҝеҗҲгӮҸгҒӣгӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгҒ§гҒҷгҖӮ1гҒӨгҒҜе…ғгҒ®зү№еҫҙз©әй–“гӮ’еҶҚж§ӢжҲҗгҒҷгӮӢгҒҹгӮҒгҒ«иЁ“з·ҙгҒ•гӮҢгҖҒгӮӮгҒҶ1гҒӨгҒҜеҶҚж§ӢжҲҗгҒ•гӮҢгҒҹзү№еҫҙгӮ’е…ҘеҠӣгҒЁгҒ—гҒҰз”»еғҸз”ҹжҲҗгӮ’иЎҢгҒ„гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮFAEгҒҜжұҺз”Ёзҡ„гҒ§гҒӮгӮҠгҖҒгҒ•гҒҫгҒ–гҒҫгҒӘиҮӘе·ұж•ҷеё«гҒӮгӮҠгӮЁгғігӮігғјгғҖпјҲдҫӢпјҡDINOгҖҒSigLIPпјүгҒ§гӮӨгғігӮ№гӮҝгғігӮ№еҢ–гҒ§гҒҚгҖҒжӢЎж•ЈгғўгғҮгғ«гӮ„жӯЈиҰҸеҢ–гғ•гғӯгғјгҒЁгҒ„гҒЈгҒҹ2гҒӨгҒ®з•°гҒӘгӮӢз”ҹжҲҗгғ•гӮЎгғҹгғӘгғјгҒ«жҺҘз¶ҡгҒ§гҒҚгҒҫгҒҷгҖӮгӮҜгғ©гӮ№жқЎд»¶д»ҳгҒҚгҒҠгӮҲгҒігғҶгӮӯгӮ№гғҲгҒӢгӮүз”»еғҸгҒёгҒ®гғҷгғігғҒгғһгғјгӮҜгҒ«гҒҠгҒ„гҒҰгҖҒFAEгҒҜеј·еҠӣгҒӘгғ‘гғ•гӮ©гғјгғһгғігӮ№гӮ’йҒ”жҲҗгҒ—гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮдҫӢгҒҲгҒ°гҖҒImageNet 256x256гҒ§гҒҜгҖҒCFGгӮ’дҪҝз”ЁгҒ—гҒҹз§ҒгҒҹгҒЎгҒ®жӢЎж•ЈгғўгғҮгғ«гҒҜгҖҒ1.29пјҲ800гӮЁгғқгғғгӮҜпјүгҒҠгӮҲгҒі1.70пјҲ80гӮЁгғқгғғгӮҜпјүгҒ®гҒ»гҒјжңҖе…Ҳз«ҜгҒ®FIDгӮ’йҒ”жҲҗгҒ—гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮCFGгҒӘгҒ—гҒ§гҒҜгҖҒFAEгҒҜ1.48пјҲ800гӮЁгғқгғғгӮҜпјүгҒҠгӮҲгҒі2.08пјҲ80гӮЁгғқгғғгӮҜпјүгҒ®жңҖе…Ҳз«ҜгҒ®FIDгҒ«йҒ”гҒ—гҖҒй«ҳе“ҒиіӘгҒӢгҒӨиҝ…йҖҹгҒӘеӯҰзҝ’гӮ’зӨәгҒ—гҒҰгҒ„гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮ ұгӮ’дҝқжҢҒгҒ—гҒӨгҒӨгҖҒ1гҒӨгҒ®гӮўгғҶгғігӮ·гғ§гғіеұӨгҒ§е®ҹзҸҫгҖӮ2гҒӨгҒ®ж·ұеұӨгғҮгӮігғјгғҖгӮ’зө„гҒҝеҗҲгӮҸгҒӣгҖҒгҒ•гҒҫгҒ–гҒҫгҒӘиҮӘе·ұж•ҷеё«гҒӮгӮҠгӮЁгғігӮігғјгғҖгҒ«еҜҫеҝңгҖӮжӢЎж•ЈгғўгғҮгғ«гӮ„жӯЈиҰҸеҢ–гғ•гғӯгғјгҒЁжҺҘз¶ҡеҸҜиғҪгҒ§гҖҒImageNetгҒ§гҒ®гғҷгғігғҒгғһгғјгӮҜгҒ«гҒҠгҒ„гҒҰе„ӘгӮҢгҒҹжҖ§иғҪгӮ’зӨәгҒҷгҖӮ

paper-entry.title: [Paper Note] X-Humanoid: Robotize Human Videos to Generate Humanoid Videos at Scale, Pei Yang+, arXiv'25, 2025.12
paper-entry.tags: ComputerVision, Dataset, Transformer, SyntheticData, action_wanted, DiffusionModel, Robotics, WorldModels, VisionLanguageActionModel, 4D (Video), EmbodiedAI, One-Line Notes, Third-Person View
paper-entry.issue-body: # URL - https://arxiv.org/abs/2512.04537 # Authors - Pei Yang - Hai Ci - Yiren Song - Mike Zheng Shou # Abstract - The advancement of embodied AI has unlocked significant potential for intelligent humanoid robots. However, progress in both Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models and world models is severely hampered by the scarcity of large-scale, diverse training data. A promising solution is to "robotize" web-scale human videos, which has been proven effective for policy training. However, these solutions mainly "overlay" robot arms to egocentric videos, which cannot handle complex full-body motions and scene occlusions in third-person videos, making them unsuitable for robotizing humans. To bridge this gap, we introduce X-Humanoid, a generative video editing approach that adapts the powerful Wan 2.2 model into a video-to-video structure and finetunes it for the human-to-humanoid translation task. This finetuning requires paired human-humanoid videos, so we designed a scalable data creation pipeline, turning community assets into 17+ hours of paired synthetic videos using Unreal Engine. We then apply our trained model to 60 hours of the Ego-Exo4D videos, generating and releasing a new large-scale dataset of over 3.6 million "robotized" humanoid video frames. Quantitative analysis and user studies confirm our method's superiority over existing baselines: 69% of users rated it best for motion consistency, and 62.1% for embodiment correctness. # Translation (by gpt-4o-mini) - гӮ’иҮӘе·ұдёӯеҝғзҡ„гҒӘеӢ•з”»гҒ«гҖҢгӮӘгғјгғҗгғјгғ¬гӮӨгҖҚгҒҷгӮӢгӮӮгҒ®гҒ§гҒӮгӮҠгҖҒз¬¬дёүиҖ…гҒ®еӢ•з”»гҒ«гҒҠгҒ‘гӮӢиӨҮйӣ‘гҒӘе…Ёиә«гҒ®еӢ•гҒҚгӮ„гӮ·гғјгғігҒ®йҒ®и”ҪгҒ«еҜҫеҮҰгҒ§гҒҚгҒҡгҖҒдәәй–“гӮ’гғӯгғңгғҶгӮЈгӮҜгӮ№еҢ–гҒҷгӮӢгҒ«гҒҜдёҚйҒ©еҲҮгҒ§гҒҷгҖӮгҒ“гҒ®гӮ®гғЈгғғгғ—гӮ’еҹӢгӮҒгӮӢгҒҹгӮҒгҒ«гҖҒз§ҒгҒҹгҒЎгҒҜX-HumanoidгӮ’е°Һе…ҘгҒ—гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮгҒ“гӮҢгҒҜгҖҒеј·еҠӣгҒӘWan 2.2гғўгғҮгғ«гӮ’еӢ•з”»гҒӢгӮүеӢ•з”»гҒёгҒ®ж§ӢйҖ гҒ«йҒ©еҝңгҒ•гҒӣгҖҒдәәй–“гҒӢгӮүгғ’гғҘгғјгғһгғҺгӮӨгғүгҒёгҒ®зҝ»иЁігӮҝгӮ№гӮҜгҒ®гҒҹгӮҒгҒ«гғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғігғҒгғҘгғјгғӢгғігӮ°гҒҷгӮӢз”ҹжҲҗзҡ„гҒӘеӢ•з”»з·ЁйӣҶгӮўгғ—гғӯгғјгғҒгҒ§гҒҷгҖӮгҒ“гҒ®гғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғігғҒгғҘгғјгғӢгғігӮ°гҒ«гҒҜгғҡгӮўгҒ®дәәй–“-гғ’гғҘгғјгғһгғҺгӮӨгғүеӢ•з”»гҒҢеҝ…иҰҒгҒӘгҒҹгӮҒгҖҒз§ҒгҒҹгҒЎгҒҜгӮігғҹгғҘгғӢгғҶгӮЈгҒ®иіҮз”ЈгӮ’дҪҝз”ЁгҒ—гҒҰгҖҒUnreal EngineгӮ’з”ЁгҒ„гҒҰ17жҷӮй–“д»ҘдёҠгҒ®гғҡгӮўеҗҲжҲҗеӢ•з”»гӮ’з”ҹжҲҗгҒҷгӮӢгӮ№гӮұгғјгғ©гғ–гғ«гҒӘгғҮгғјгӮҝдҪңжҲҗгғ‘гӮӨгғ—гғ©гӮӨгғігӮ’иЁӯиЁҲгҒ—гҒҫгҒ—гҒҹгҖӮж¬ЎгҒ«гҖҒз§ҒгҒҹгҒЎгҒҜиЁ“з·ҙгҒ—гҒҹгғўгғҮгғ«гӮ’60жҷӮй–“гҒ®Ego-Exo4DеӢ•з”»гҒ«йҒ©з”ЁгҒ—гҖҒ360дёҮд»ҘдёҠгҒ®гҖҢгғӯгғңгғҶгӮЈгӮҜгӮ№еҢ–гҖҚгҒ•гӮҢгҒҹгғ’гғҘгғјгғһгғҺгӮӨгғүеӢ•з”»гғ•гғ¬гғјгғ гҒ®ж–°гҒ—гҒ„еӨ§иҰҸжЁЎгғҮгғјгӮҝгӮ»гғғгғҲгӮ’з”ҹжҲҗгҒ—е…¬й–ӢгҒ—гҒҫгҒ—гҒҹгҖӮе®ҡйҮҸзҡ„еҲҶжһҗгҒЁгғҰгғјгӮ¶гғјиӘҝжҹ»гҒҜгҖҒз§ҒгҒҹгҒЎгҒ®жүӢжі•гҒҢж—ўеӯҳгҒ®гғҷгғјгӮ№гғ©гӮӨгғігӮ’дёҠеӣһгӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгӮ’зўәиӘҚгҒ—гҒҰгҒ„гҒҫгҒҷпјҡ69%гҒ®гғҰгғјгӮ¶гғјгҒҢеӢ•гҒҚгҒ®дёҖиІ«жҖ§гҒ«гҒҠгҒ„гҒҰжңҖгӮӮе„ӘгӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгҒЁи©•дҫЎгҒ—гҖҒ62.1%гҒҢе…·зҸҫеҢ–гҒ®жӯЈзўәгҒ•гҒ«гҒҠгҒ„гҒҰжңҖгӮӮе„ӘгӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгҒЁи©•дҫЎгҒ—гҒҫгҒ—гҒҹгҖӮ # Summary (by gpt-4o-mini) - X-HumanoidгҒҜгҖҒеӢ•з”»гҒӢгӮүеӢ•з”»гҒёгҒ®з”ҹжҲҗзҡ„гҒӘз·ЁйӣҶгӮўгғ—гғӯгғјгғҒгӮ’з”ЁгҒ„гҒҰгҖҒдәәй–“гҒӢгӮүгғ’гғҘгғјгғһгғҺгӮӨгғүгҒёгҒ®зҝ»иЁігӮ’е®ҹзҸҫгҒҷгӮӢгғўгғҮгғ«гҒ§гҒҷгҖӮUnreal EngineгӮ’жҙ»з”ЁгҒ—гҖҒ17жҷӮй–“д»ҘдёҠгҒ®гғҡгӮўеҗҲжҲҗеӢ•з”»гӮ’з”ҹжҲҗгҒҷгӮӢгғҮгғјгӮҝдҪңжҲҗгғ‘гӮӨгғ—гғ©гӮӨгғігӮ’иЁӯиЁҲгҒ—гҖҒ60жҷӮй–“гҒ®Ego-Exo4DеӢ•з”»гӮ’з”ЁгҒ„гҒҰ360дёҮд»ҘдёҠгҒ®гҖҢгғӯгғңгғҶгӮЈгӮҜгӮ№еҢ–гҖҚгҒ•гӮҢгҒҹгғ’гғҘгғјгғһгғҺгӮӨгғүеӢ•з”»гғ•гғ¬гғјгғ гӮ’з”ҹжҲҗгҒ—гҒҫгҒ—гҒҹгҖӮе®ҡйҮҸзҡ„еҲҶжһҗгҒЁгғҰгғјгӮ¶гғјиӘҝжҹ»гҒ«гӮҲгӮҠгҖҒ69%гҒ®гғҰгғјгӮ¶гғјгҒҢеӢ•гҒҚгҒ®дёҖиІ«жҖ§гҒ§жңҖгӮӮе„ӘгӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгҒЁи©•дҫЎгҒ—гҖҒ62.1%гҒҢе…·зҸҫеҢ–гҒ®жӯЈзўәгҒ•гҒ§жңҖгӮӮе„ӘгӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгҒЁи©•дҫЎгҒ—гҒҫгҒ—гҒҹгҖӮ

maintainer-comment-text.p:
ж—ўеӯҳз ”з©¶гҒҜдё»иҰіиҰ–зӮ№гҒ®еӢ•з”»гҒ«гҒҠгҒ‘гӮӢдәәгҒ®и…•гӮ’гғӯгғңгғғгғҲгӮўгғјгғ гҒ«гғ«гғјгғ«гғҷгғјгӮ№гҒ§зҪ®гҒҚжҸӣгҒҲгӮӢгҒӘгҒ©гҒ®ж–№жі•гҒ§еӢ•з”»гӮ’гӮӘгғјгғҗгғ¬гӮӨгҒҷгӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгҒ§data scarcityгҒ®е•ҸйЎҢгҒ«еҜҫеҮҰгҒ—гҒҰгҒҚгҒҰгҒҠгӮҠгҖҒгҒ“гӮҢгҒҜжңүжңӣгҒӘгӮўгғ—гғӯгғјгғҒгҒ гҒҢгҖҒз¬¬дёүиҖ…иҰ–зӮ№гҒ®еӢ•з”»гҒҜгҒ—гҒ°гҒ—гҒ°гӮҲгӮҠиӨҮйӣ‘пјҲе…Ёиә«гҒҢеҶҷгӮҠгҖҒиғҢжҷҜгҒҢеӢ•зҡ„гҒ«еӨүеҢ–гҒ—йҒ®и”ҪгҒ«йҡ гӮҢгҒҹгӮҠгӮӮгҒҷгӮӢпјүгҒ§иӘІйЎҢгҒҢгҒӮгӮӢгҖӮгҒ“гҒ®гҒҹгӮҒгҖҒз¬¬дёүиҖ…иҰ–зӮ№гҒ§гҒ®еӢ•з”»гӮ’дәәй–“гҒӢгӮүгғ’гғҘгғјгғһгғҺгӮӨгғүгҒ«зҪ®жҸӣгҒҷгӮӢгғўгғҮгғ«гӮ’еӯҰзҝ’[^1]гҒ—пјҲеј·еҠӣгҒӘvideo editingгғўгғҮгғ«гҒ§гӮӮгҒ“гҒ®зӮ№гҒҜгҒҫгҒ иӢҰжҲҰгҒҷгӮӢгӮҝгӮ№гӮҜгҒЁгҒ®гҒ“гҒЁпјүгҖҒз§Ғз”ҹжҙ»гҒ«гҒҠгҒ‘гӮӢдәәй–“гҒ®еӢ•з”»гӮ’гғ’гғҘгғјгғһгғҺгӮӨгғүгҒ«зҪ®гҒҚжҸӣгҒҲгҒҰгғҮгғјгӮҝгӮ’еҗҲжҲҗгҒҷгӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгҒ§гғӯгғңгғғгғҲгҒ®гғқгғӘгӮ·гғјгӮ„дё–з•ҢгғўгғҮгғ«гҒ®еӯҰзҝ’гғҮгғјгӮҝдёҚи¶ігӮ’иЈңгҒ„гҒҫгҒҷгҖҒгҒЁгҒ„гҒҶи©ұгҒ«иҰӢгҒҲгӮӢгҖӮ
[^1]: гҒ“гҒ®йғЁеҲҶгҒ®еӯҰзҝ’гғҮгғјгӮҝгҒҜUnreal EngineгӮ’з”ЁгҒ„гҒҰ17+жҷӮй–“гҒ«еҸҠгҒ¶дәәй–“-гғ’гғҘгғјгғһгғҺгӮӨгғүгғҡгӮўгҒ®еӢ•з”»гӮ’еҗҲжҲҗ

пјҲд»ҘдёӢChatgptгҒЁгҒ®е•Ҹзӯ”гҒ«гӮҲгӮҠеҫ—гҒҹжғ…е ұгҒӘгҒ®гҒ§гғҸгғ«гӮ·гғҚгғјгӮ·гғ§гғігҒ®жҒҗгӮҢгҒҢгҒӮгӮҠгҒҫгҒҷпјү
дё»иҰіиҰ–зӮ№гҒ§гҒ®дәәй–“гҒ®и…•гӮ’гғӯгғңгғғгғҲгӮўгғјгғ гҒ«зҪ®гҒҚжҸӣгҒҲгҒҰеӯҰзҝ’гғҮгғјгӮҝгӮ’еҗҲжҲҗгҒҷгӮӢгҒЁгҒ„гҒҶгҒ®гҒҜж°—жҢҒгҒЎгҒҢеҲҶгҒӢгӮҠгӮ„гҒҷгҒӢгҒЈгҒҹгҒ®гҒ гҒҢпјҲпјқдәәй–“гҒ®и…•гҒЁе®ҹйҡӣгҒ«гғӯгғңгғғгғҲиҮӘиә«гҒҢгӮ«гғЎгғ©гӮ’йҖҡгҒҳгҒҰиҰӢгӮӢиҮӘеҲҶгҒ®и…•гҒҜеҪўзҠ¶гҒҢйҒ•гҒҶгҒҹгӮҒеӯҰзҝ’жҷӮгҒЁйҒӢз”ЁжҷӮгҒ«gapгҒҢз”ҹгҒҳгӮӢпјүгҖҒгҒӘгҒңз¬¬дёүиҖ…иҰ–зӮ№гҒ§гҒ®гҒ“гҒ®гӮҲгҒҶгҒӘHuman-Humanoid gapгӮ’еҹӢгӮҒгҒҹеӯҰзҝ’гғҮгғјгӮҝгҒҢеҝ…иҰҒгҒӘгҒ®гҒӢгҖҒгҒЁгҒ„гҒҶи©ұгҒҜгҒ–гғјгҒЈгҒЁи«–ж–ҮгӮ’иҰӢгҒҹйҷҗгӮҠжӣёгҒ„гҒҰгҒҠгӮүгҒҡй–ҖеӨ–жјўгҒ®з§ҒгҒ§гҒҜгӮҸгҒӢгӮүгҒӘгҒӢгҒЈгҒҹгҒ®гҒ§гҖҒChatgptгӮ„GeminiгҒ«гҒҚгҒ„гҒҰгҒҝгҒҹгҖӮLLMгҒ®еҝңзӯ”гҒ«гӮҲгӮӢгҒЁ
- дё»иҰіиҰ–зӮ№гҒ§гҒ®еӢ•з”»гҒ«гҒҜйҷҗгӮҠгҒҢгҒӮгӮҠгҖҒз¬¬дёүиҖ…иҰ–зӮ№гҒ§гҒ®еӢ•з”»гҒ®ж–№гҒҢеҚҳзҙ”гҒ«гғҮгғјгӮҝйҮҸгҒҢеӨҡгҒ„
- дё»иҰіиҰ–зӮ№еӢ•з”»гҒ§гҒҜиҰӢгҒҲгӮӢзҜ„еӣІгҒҢйҷҗе®ҡзҡ„гҒ§гҒӮгӮҠгҖҒгҒҹгҒЁгҒҲгҒ°гғӯгғңгғғгғҲгҒ«зү№е®ҡгҒ®еӢ•дҪңгӮ’еӯҰзҝ’гҒ•гҒӣгҒҹгҒ„гҒЁгҒҚгҒ«гҖҒе…Ёиә«еӢ•дҪңгӮ„иғҢжҷҜгҒ®еӢ•гҒҚгҖҒзү©дҪ“гҒЁгҒ®дҪҚзҪ®й–ўдҝӮгҒӘгҒ©гҒҜгӮҸгҒӢгӮүгҒӘгҒ„гҖӮ
- гғӯгғңгғғгғҲгҒҢе®ҹйҡӣгҒ«еҫ—гӮӢиҰ–з•ҢгӮӮгғӯгғңгғғгғҲгҒӢгӮүиҰӢгҒҹжҷӮгҒ®дё»иҰіиҰ–зӮ№гҒ§гҒӮгӮӢгҒҢгҖҒгҒқгӮҢгҒЁгҒҜеҲҘгҒ®и©ұгҒЁгҒ—гҒҰгҒ“гҒ®гӮҲгҒҶгҒӘз¬¬дёүиҖ…иҰ–зӮ№гҒҢгғӯгғңгғғгғҲгҒҢеӨҡж§ҳгҒӘгӮҝгӮ№гӮҜгӮ’еӯҰгҒ¶гҒЁгҒҚгҒ«е…Ёиә«гҒҢеҶҷгҒЈгҒҰгҒ„гӮӢеӢ•з”»гҒҜжңүз”ЁгҒ§гҒӮгӮӢгҒӢпјҲгӮҝгӮ№гӮҜгҖҒж„ҸеӣігҖҒиЎҢеӢ•гҒ®йҒёжҠһгғ‘гӮҝгғјгғігҒӘгҒ©гҒ®еӢ•дҪңгҒ®ж„Ҹе‘іжғ…е ұгӮ’еӯҰгҒ¶пјүгҖӮгҒҫгҒҹгҖҒз¬¬дёүиҖ…иҰ–зӮ№еӢ•з”»гӮ’гғӯгғңгғғгғҲгҒ®иҰ–зӮ№гҒ«еӨүжҸӣгҒҷгӮӢгӮҲгҒҶгҒӘгғўгғҮгғ«гӮ’дҪңгӮӢгҒҹгӮҒгҒ«гӮӮгҒ“гҒ®гӮҲгҒҶгҒӘгғҮгғјгӮҝгҒҜеҝ…иҰҒгҒ§гҖҒгҒ“гӮҢгҒ«гӮҲгӮҠгғӯгғңгғғгғҲгҒҜз¬¬дёүиҖ…иҰ–зӮ№гҒ®дәәй–“еӢ•з”»гҒӢгӮүеӯҰгҒігҖҒжңҖзөӮзҡ„гҒ«гҒқгӮҢгӮүгӮ’иҮӘеҲҶгҒ®дё»иҰіиҰ–зӮ№гҒ«еҜҫеҝңгҒҷгӮӢиЎЁзҸҫгҒЁгҒ—гҒҰеӯҰзҝ’пјҲretargetгҒЁе‘јгҒ¶гӮүгҒ—гҒ„пјүгҒ§гҒҚгӮӢгҖӮ

вҶ‘гҒ®LLMгҒӢгӮүгҒ®жғ…е ұгҒҜеҰҘеҪ“гҒӘгӮҲгҒҶгҒ«ж„ҹгҒҳгӮӢгҖӮ
гҒҫгҒӮгҒӮгҒЁгҒҜгҖҒгҒқгӮӮгҒқгӮӮгҖҒгғӯгғңгғғгғҲгҒҢжәўгӮҢгҒӢгҒҲгӮӢдё–з•ҢгҒ«гҒӘгҒЈгҒҹгҒЁгҒҚгҒ«гҖҒгғӯгғңгғғгғҲгҒҢеҶҷгҒЈгҒҰгҒ„гӮӢеӯҰзҝ’гғҮгғјгӮҝгҒҢгҒӘгҒ„гҒЁгҒҫгҒҡгҒ„гӮҲгҒӯгҖҒгҒЁгҒ„гҒҶгҒ®гӮӮе°ҶжқҘзҡ„гҒ«гҒҜгҒӮгӮӢгҒ®гҒӢгҒӘгҒЁгҒ„гҒҶж„ҹжғігҖӮ

paper-entry.title: [Paper Note] On the Closed-Form of Flow Matching: Generalization Does Not Arise from Target Stochasticity, Quentin Bertrand+, arXiv'25, 2025.06
paper-entry.tags: ComputerVision, DiffusionModel, FlowMatching
paper-entry.issue-body: # URL - https://arxiv.org/abs/2506.03719 # Authors - Quentin Bertrand - Anne Gagneux - Mathurin Massias - RГ©mi Emonet # Abstract - Modern deep generative models can now produce high-quality synthetic samples that are often indistinguishable from real training data. A growing body of research aims to understand why recent methods, such as diffusion and flow matching techniques, generalize so effectively. Among the proposed explanations are the inductive biases of deep learning architectures and the stochastic nature of the conditional flow matching loss. In this work, we rule out the noisy nature of the loss as a key factor driving generalization in flow matching. First, we empirically show that in high-dimensional settings, the stochastic and closed-form versions of the flow matching loss yield nearly equivalent losses. Then, using state-of-the-art flow matching models on standard image datasets, we demonstrate that both variants achieve comparable statistical performance, with the surprising observation that using the closed-form can even improve performance. # Translation (by gpt-4o-mini) - зҸҫд»ЈгҒ®ж·ұеұӨз”ҹжҲҗгғўгғҮгғ«гҒҜгҖҒе®ҹйҡӣгҒ®гғҲгғ¬гғјгғӢгғігӮ°гғҮгғјгӮҝгҒЁеҢәеҲҘгҒҢгҒӨгҒӢгҒӘгҒ„й«ҳе“ҒиіӘгҒӘеҗҲжҲҗгӮөгғігғ—гғ«гӮ’з”ҹжҲҗгҒ§гҒҚгӮӢгӮҲгҒҶгҒ«гҒӘгҒЈгҒҰгҒ„гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮжңҖиҝ‘гҒ®жүӢжі•гҖҒзү№гҒ«жӢЎж•Јжі•гӮ„гғ•гғӯгғјгғһгғғгғҒгғігӮ°жҠҖиЎ“гҒҢгҒӘгҒңгҒ“гӮҢгҒ»гҒ©еҠ№жһңзҡ„гҒ«дёҖиҲ¬еҢ–гҒҷгӮӢгҒ®гҒӢгӮ’зҗҶи§ЈгҒҷгӮӢгҒҹгӮҒгҒ®з ”з©¶гҒҢеў—гҒҲгҒҰгҒ„гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮжҸҗжЎҲгҒ•гӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гӮӢиӘ¬жҳҺгҒ®дёӯгҒ«гҒҜгҖҒж·ұеұӨеӯҰзҝ’гӮўгғјгӮӯгғҶгӮҜгғҒгғЈгҒ®её°зҙҚзҡ„гғҗгӮӨгӮўгӮ№гӮ„жқЎд»¶д»ҳгҒҚгғ•гғӯгғјгғһгғғгғҒгғігӮ°жҗҚеӨұгҒ®зўәзҺҮзҡ„жҖ§иіӘгҒҢгҒӮгӮҠгҒҫгҒҷгҖӮжң¬з ”з©¶гҒ§гҒҜгҖҒгғ•гғӯгғјгғһгғғгғҒгғігӮ°гҒ«гҒҠгҒ‘гӮӢдёҖиҲ¬еҢ–гӮ’дҝғйҖІгҒҷгӮӢйҮҚиҰҒгҒӘиҰҒеӣ гҒЁгҒ—гҒҰгҖҒжҗҚеӨұгҒ®гғҺгӮӨгӮәгҒ®жҖ§иіӘгӮ’жҺ’йҷӨгҒ—гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮгҒҫгҒҡгҖҒй«ҳж¬Ўе…ғгҒ®иЁӯе®ҡгҒ«гҒҠгҒ„гҒҰгҖҒгғ•гғӯгғјгғһгғғгғҒгғігӮ°жҗҚеӨұгҒ®зўәзҺҮзҡ„гғҗгғјгӮёгғ§гғігҒЁй–үеҪўејҸгғҗгғјгӮёгғ§гғігҒҢгҒ»гҒјеҗҢзӯүгҒ®жҗҚеӨұгӮ’гӮӮгҒҹгӮүгҒҷгҒ“гҒЁгӮ’е®ҹиЁјзҡ„гҒ«зӨәгҒ—гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮж¬ЎгҒ«гҖҒжЁҷжә–зҡ„гҒӘз”»еғҸгғҮгғјгӮҝгӮ»гғғгғҲгҒ«гҒҠгҒ‘гӮӢжңҖе…Ҳз«ҜгҒ®гғ•гғӯгғјгғһгғғгғҒгғігӮ°гғўгғҮгғ«гӮ’дҪҝз”ЁгҒ—гҒҰгҖҒдёЎж–№гҒ®гғҗгғӘгӮЁгғјгӮ·гғ§гғігҒҢжҜ”ијғеҸҜиғҪгҒӘзөұиЁҲзҡ„гғ‘гғ•гӮ©гғјгғһгғігӮ№гӮ’йҒ”жҲҗгҒҷгӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгӮ’зӨәгҒ—гҖҒй–үеҪўејҸгӮ’дҪҝз”ЁгҒҷгӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгҒ§гғ‘гғ•гӮ©гғјгғһгғігӮ№гҒҢеҗ‘дёҠгҒҷгӮӢгҒЁгҒ„гҒҶй©ҡгҒҸгҒ№гҒҚиҰіеҜҹгӮ’иЎҢгҒ„гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮ # Summary (by gpt-4o-mini) - й–ўйҖЈ:
- [Paper Note] Selective Underfitting in Diffusion Models, Kiwhan Song+, arXiv'25, 2025.10

paper-entry.title: [Paper Note] Why Diffusion Models Don't Memorize: The Role of Implicit Dynamical Regularization in Training, Tony Bonnaire+, NeurIPS'25 Best Paper Awards, 2025.05
paper-entry.tags: Analysis, MachineLearning, DiffusionModel, NeurIPS, Memorization, Generalization
paper-entry.issue-body: # URL - https://arxiv.org/abs/2505.17638 # Authors - Tony Bonnaire - RaphaГ«l Urfin - Giulio Biroli - Marc MГ©zard # Abstract - Diffusion models have achieved remarkable success across a wide range of generative tasks. A key challenge is understanding the mechanisms that prevent their memorization of training data and allow generalization. In this work, we investigate the role of the training dynamics in the transition from generalization to memorization. Through extensive experiments and theoretical analysis, we identify two distinct timescales: an early time $П„_\mathrm{gen}$ at which models begin to generate high-quality samples, and a later time $П„_\mathrm{mem}$ beyond which memorization emerges. Crucially, we find that $П„_\mathrm{mem}$ increases linearly with the training set size $n$, while $П„_\mathrm{gen}$ remains constant. This creates a growing window of training times with $n$ where models generalize effectively, despite showing strong memorization if training continues beyond it. It is only when $n$ becomes larger than a model-dependent threshold that overfitting disappears at infinite training times. These findings reveal a form of implicit dynamical regularization in the training dynamics, which allow to avoid memorization even in highly overparameterized settings. Our results are supported by numerical experiments with standard U-Net architectures on realistic and synthetic datasets, and by a theoretical analysis using a tractable random features model studied in the high-dimensional limit. # Translation (by gpt-4o-mini) - жӢЎж•ЈгғўгғҮгғ«гҒҜгҖҒгҒ•гҒҫгҒ–гҒҫгҒӘз”ҹжҲҗгӮҝгӮ№гӮҜгҒ«гҒҠгҒ„гҒҰйЎ•и‘—гҒӘжҲҗеҠҹгӮ’еҸҺгӮҒгҒҰгҒ„гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮйҮҚиҰҒгҒӘиӘІйЎҢгҒҜгҖҒгғҲгғ¬гғјгғӢгғігӮ°гғҮгғјгӮҝгҒ®иЁҳжҶ¶гӮ’йҳІгҒҺгҖҒдёҖиҲ¬еҢ–гӮ’еҸҜиғҪгҒ«гҒҷгӮӢгғЎгӮ«гғӢгӮәгғ гӮ’зҗҶи§ЈгҒҷгӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгҒ§гҒҷгҖӮжң¬з ”з©¶гҒ§гҒҜгҖҒдёҖиҲ¬еҢ–гҒӢгӮүиЁҳжҶ¶гҒёгҒ®з§»иЎҢгҒ«гҒҠгҒ‘гӮӢгғҲгғ¬гғјгғӢгғігӮ°гғҖгӮӨгғҠгғҹгӮҜгӮ№гҒ®еҪ№еүІгӮ’иӘҝжҹ»гҒ—гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮеәғзҜ„гҒӘе®ҹйЁ“гҒЁзҗҶи«–зҡ„еҲҶжһҗгӮ’йҖҡгҒҳгҒҰгҖҒгғўгғҮгғ«гҒҢй«ҳе“ҒиіӘгҒӘгӮөгғігғ—гғ«гӮ’з”ҹжҲҗгҒ—е§ӢгӮҒгӮӢеҲқжңҹгҒ®жҷӮй–“гӮ№гӮұгғјгғ« $П„_\mathrm{gen}$ гҒЁгҖҒиЁҳжҶ¶гҒҢзҸҫгӮҢгӮӢеҫҢгҒ®жҷӮй–“гӮ№гӮұгғјгғ« $П„_\mathrm{mem}$ гҒ®2гҒӨгҒ®з•°гҒӘгӮӢжҷӮй–“гӮ№гӮұгғјгғ«гӮ’зү№е®ҡгҒ—гҒҫгҒ—гҒҹгҖӮйҮҚиҰҒгҒӘгҒ“гҒЁгҒ«гҖҒ$П„_\mathrm{mem}$ гҒҜгғҲгғ¬гғјгғӢгғігӮ°гӮ»гғғгғҲгҒ®гӮөгӮӨгӮә $n$ гҒ«еҜҫгҒ—гҒҰз·ҡеҪўгҒ«еў—еҠ гҒҷгӮӢдёҖж–№гҒ§гҖҒ$П„_\mathrm{gen}$ гҒҜдёҖе®ҡгҒ®гҒҫгҒҫгҒ§гҒҷгҖӮгҒ“гӮҢгҒ«гӮҲгӮҠгҖҒгғўгғҮгғ«гҒҢеҠ№жһңзҡ„гҒ«дёҖиҲ¬еҢ–гҒҷгӮӢгғҲгғ¬гғјгғӢгғігӮ°жҷӮй–“гҒ®гӮҰгӮЈгғігғүгӮҰгҒҢ $n$ гҒ«еҝңгҒҳгҒҰжӢЎеӨ§гҒ—гҖҒгғҲгғ¬гғјгғӢгғігӮ°гҒҢгҒқгӮҢгӮ’и¶…гҒҲгҒҰз¶ҡгҒҸгҒЁеј·гҒ„иЁҳжҶ¶гӮ’зӨәгҒҷгҒ«гӮӮгҒӢгҒӢгӮҸгӮүгҒҡгҖҒдёҖиҲ¬еҢ–гҒҢеҸҜиғҪгҒ«гҒӘгӮҠгҒҫгҒҷгҖӮ$n$ гҒҢгғўгғҮгғ«дҫқеӯҳгҒ®й–ҫеҖӨгӮ’и¶…гҒҲгӮӢгҒЁгҖҒз„ЎйҷҗгҒ®гғҲгғ¬гғјгғӢгғігӮ°жҷӮй–“гҒ«гҒҠгҒ„гҒҰйҒҺеӯҰзҝ’гҒҢж¶ҲеӨұгҒ—гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮгҒ“гӮҢгӮүгҒ®зҷәиҰӢгҒҜгҖҒгғҲгғ¬гғјгғӢгғігӮ°гғҖгӮӨгғҠгғҹгӮҜгӮ№гҒ«гҒҠгҒ‘гӮӢжҡ—й»ҷгҒ®еӢ•зҡ„жӯЈеүҮеҢ–гҒ®дёҖеҪўж…ӢгӮ’жҳҺгӮүгҒӢгҒ«гҒ—гҖҒй«ҳеәҰгҒ«йҒҺеү°гғ‘гғ©гғЎгғјгӮҝеҢ–гҒ•гӮҢгҒҹиЁӯе®ҡгҒ«гҒҠгҒ„гҒҰгӮӮиЁҳжҶ¶гӮ’еӣһйҒҝгҒҷгӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгӮ’еҸҜиғҪгҒ«гҒ—гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮз§ҒгҒҹгҒЎгҒ®зөҗжһңгҒҜгҖҒзҸҫе®ҹзҡ„гҒҠгӮҲгҒіеҗҲжҲҗгғҮгғјгӮҝгӮ»гғғгғҲгҒ«еҜҫгҒҷгӮӢжЁҷжә–зҡ„гҒӘU-NetгӮўгғјгӮӯгғҶгӮҜгғҒгғЈгӮ’з”ЁгҒ„гҒҹж•°еҖӨе®ҹйЁ“гҒЁгҖҒй«ҳж¬Ўе…ғеҲ¶йҷҗгҒ§з ”з©¶гҒ•гӮҢгҒҹжүұгҒ„гӮ„гҒҷгҒ„гғ©гғігғҖгғ зү№еҫҙгғўгғҮгғ«гӮ’з”ЁгҒ„гҒҹзҗҶи«–зҡ„еҲҶжһҗгҒ«гӮҲгҒЈгҒҰж”ҜжҢҒгҒ•гӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮ # Summary (by gpt-4o-mini) - жӢЎж•ЈгғўгғҮгғ«гҒ®гғҲгғ¬гғјгғӢгғігӮ°гғҖгӮӨгғҠгғҹгӮҜгӮ№гӮ’иӘҝжҹ»гҒ—гҖҒдёҖиҲ¬еҢ–гҒӢгӮүиЁҳжҶ¶гҒёгҒ®з§»иЎҢгҒ«гҒҠгҒ‘гӮӢ2гҒӨгҒ®жҷӮй–“гӮ№гӮұгғјгғ«пјҲ$П„_\mathrm{gen}$гҒЁ$П„_\mathrm{mem}$пјүгӮ’зү№е®ҡгҖӮ$П„_\mathrm{mem}$гҒҜгғҲгғ¬гғјгғӢгғігӮ°гӮ»гғғгғҲгҒ®гӮөгӮӨгӮәгҒ«з·ҡеҪўгҒ«еў—еҠ гҒ—гҖҒдёҖиҲ¬еҢ–гҒҢеҸҜиғҪгҒӘгғҲгғ¬гғјгғӢгғігӮ°жҷӮй–“гҒ®гӮҰгӮЈгғігғүгӮҰгҒҢжӢЎеӨ§гҒҷгӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгӮ’зӨәгҒҷгҖӮгҒ“гӮҢгҒ«гӮҲгӮҠгҖҒйҒҺеӯҰзҝ’гҒҢж¶ҲеӨұгҒҷгӮӢй–ҫеҖӨгҒҢеӯҳеңЁгҒ—гҖҒиЁҳжҶ¶гӮ’еӣһйҒҝгҒ§гҒҚгӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгҒҢжҳҺгӮүгҒӢгҒ«гҖӮе®ҹйЁ“гҒЁзҗҶи«–еҲҶжһҗгҒ«гӮҲгӮҠзөҗжһңгҒҢж”ҜжҢҒгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢгҖӮ

paper-entry.title: [Paper Note] PixelDiT: Pixel Diffusion Transformers for Image Generation, Yongsheng Yu+, arXiv'25, 2025.11
paper-entry.tags: ComputerVision, Transformer, DiffusionModel, TextToImageGeneration, ImageSynthesis, Pixel-based
paper-entry.issue-body: # URL - https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.20645 # Authors - Yongsheng Yu - Wei Xiong - Weili Nie - Yichen Sheng - Shiqiu Liu - Jiebo Luo # Abstract - Latent-space modeling has been the standard for Diffusion Transformers (DiTs). However, it relies on a two-stage pipeline where the pretrained autoencoder introduces lossy reconstruction, leading to error accumulation while hindering joint optimization. To address these issues, we propose PixelDiT, a single-stage, end-to-end model that eliminates the need for the autoencoder and learns the diffusion process directly in the pixel space. PixelDiT adopts a fully transformer-based architecture shaped by a dual-level design: a patch-level DiT that captures global semantics and a pixel-level DiT that refines texture details, enabling efficient training of a pixel-space diffusion model while preserving fine details. Our analysis reveals that effective pixel-level token modeling is essential to the success of pixel diffusion. PixelDiT achieves 1.61 FID on ImageNet 256x256, surpassing existing pixel generative models by a large margin. We further extend PixelDiT to text-to-image generation and pretrain it at the 1024x1024 resolution in pixel space. It achieves 0.74 on GenEval and 83.5 on DPG-bench, approaching the best latent diffusion models. # Translation (by gpt-4o-mini) - жҪңеңЁз©әй–“гғўгғҮгғ«гҒҜгҖҒDiffusion TransformersпјҲDiTsпјүгҒ®жЁҷжә–гҒЁгҒӘгҒЈгҒҰгҒ„гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮгҒ—гҒӢгҒ—гҖҒгҒ“гӮҢгҒҜдәӢеүҚеӯҰзҝ’гҒ•гӮҢгҒҹгӮӘгғјгғҲгӮЁгғігӮігғјгғҖгғјгҒҢжҗҚеӨұгҒ®гҒӮгӮӢеҶҚж§ӢжҲҗгӮ’е°Һе…ҘгҒҷгӮӢдәҢж®өйҡҺгҒ®гғ‘гӮӨгғ—гғ©гӮӨгғігҒ«дҫқеӯҳгҒ—гҒҰгҒҠгӮҠгҖҒгӮЁгғ©гғјгҒ®и“„з©ҚгӮ’еј•гҒҚиө·гҒ“гҒ—гҖҒе…ұеҗҢжңҖйҒ©еҢ–гӮ’еҰЁгҒ’гҒҰгҒ„гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮгҒ“гӮҢгӮүгҒ®е•ҸйЎҢгҒ«еҜҫеҮҰгҒҷгӮӢгҒҹгӮҒгҒ«гҖҒжҲ‘гҖ…гҒҜPixelDiTгӮ’жҸҗжЎҲгҒ—гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮгҒ“гӮҢгҒҜгҖҒгӮӘгғјгғҲгӮЁгғігӮігғјгғҖгғјгҒ®еҝ…иҰҒжҖ§гӮ’жҺ’йҷӨгҒ—гҖҒгғ”гӮҜгӮ»гғ«з©әй–“гҒ§жӢЎж•Јгғ—гғӯгӮ»гӮ№гӮ’зӣҙжҺҘеӯҰзҝ’гҒҷгӮӢеҚҳдёҖж®өйҡҺгҒ®гӮЁгғігғүгғ„гғјгӮЁгғігғүгғўгғҮгғ«гҒ§гҒҷгҖӮPixelDiTгҒҜгҖҒгӮ°гғӯгғјгғҗгғ«гҒӘгӮ»гғһгғігғҶгӮЈгӮҜгӮ№гӮ’жҚүгҒҲгӮӢгғ‘гғғгғҒгғ¬гғҷгғ«гҒ®DiTгҒЁгҖҒгғҶгӮҜгӮ№гғҒгғЈгҒ®и©ізҙ°гӮ’жҙ—з·ҙгҒҷгӮӢгғ”гӮҜгӮ»гғ«гғ¬гғҷгғ«гҒ®DiTгҒЁгҒ„гҒҶдәҢйҮҚгғ¬гғҷгғ«гҒ®гғҮгӮ¶гӮӨгғігҒ«гӮҲгҒЈгҒҰеҪўдҪңгӮүгӮҢгҒҹе®Ңе…ЁгҒӘгғҲгғ©гғігӮ№гғ•гӮ©гғјгғһгғјгғҷгғјгӮ№гҒ®гӮўгғјгӮӯгғҶгӮҜгғҒгғЈгӮ’жҺЎз”ЁгҒ—гҒҰгҒҠгӮҠгҖҒзҙ°йғЁгӮ’дҝқжҢҒгҒ—гҒӘгҒҢгӮүгғ”гӮҜгӮ»гғ«з©әй–“гҒ®жӢЎж•ЈгғўгғҮгғ«гҒ®еҠ№зҺҮзҡ„гҒӘгғҲгғ¬гғјгғӢгғігӮ°гӮ’еҸҜиғҪгҒ«гҒ—гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮжҲ‘гҖ…гҒ®еҲҶжһҗгҒҜгҖҒеҠ№жһңзҡ„гҒӘгғ”гӮҜгӮ»гғ«гғ¬гғҷгғ«гҒ®гғҲгғјгӮҜгғігғўгғҮгғӘгғігӮ°гҒҢгғ”гӮҜгӮ»гғ«жӢЎж•ЈгҒ®жҲҗеҠҹгҒ«дёҚеҸҜж¬ гҒ§гҒӮгӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгӮ’жҳҺгӮүгҒӢгҒ«гҒ—гҒҰгҒ„гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮPixelDiTгҒҜгҖҒImageNet 256x256гҒ§1.61гҒ®FIDгӮ’йҒ”жҲҗгҒ—гҖҒж—ўеӯҳгҒ®гғ”гӮҜгӮ»гғ«з”ҹжҲҗгғўгғҮгғ«гӮ’еӨ§гҒҚгҒҸдёҠеӣһгӮҠгҒҫгҒҷгҖӮгҒ•гӮүгҒ«гҖҒPixelDiTгӮ’гғҶгӮӯгӮ№гғҲгҒӢгӮүз”»еғҸгҒёгҒ®з”ҹжҲҗгҒ«жӢЎејөгҒ—гҖҒгғ”гӮҜгӮ»гғ«з©әй–“гҒ§1024x1024гҒ®и§ЈеғҸеәҰгҒ§дәӢеүҚеӯҰзҝ’гӮ’иЎҢгҒ„гҒҫгҒ—гҒҹгҖӮгҒ“гӮҢгҒ«гӮҲгӮҠгҖҒGenEvalгҒ§0.74гҖҒDPG-benchгҒ§83.5гӮ’йҒ”жҲҗгҒ—гҖҒжңҖиүҜгҒ®жҪңеңЁжӢЎж•ЈгғўгғҮгғ«гҒ«иҝ‘гҒҘгҒ„гҒҰгҒ„гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮ # Summary (by gpt-4o-mini) - PixelDiTгҒҜгҖҒгӮӘгғјгғҲгӮЁгғігӮігғјгғҖгғјгӮ’жҺ’йҷӨгҒ—гҖҒгғ”гӮҜгӮ»гғ«з©әй–“гҒ§гҒ®жӢЎж•Јгғ—гғӯгӮ»гӮ№гӮ’зӣҙжҺҘеӯҰзҝ’гҒҷгӮӢгӮЁгғігғүгғ„гғјгӮЁгғігғүгғўгғҮгғ«гҒ§гҒӮгӮӢгҖӮгӮ°гғӯгғјгғҗгғ«гҒӘгӮ»гғһгғігғҶгӮЈгӮҜгӮ№гҒЁгғҶгӮҜгӮ№гғҒгғЈгҒ®и©ізҙ°гӮ’жҚүгҒҲгӮӢдәҢйҮҚгғ¬гғҷгғ«гҒ®гғҲгғ©гғігӮ№гғ•гӮ©гғјгғһгғјгӮўгғјгӮӯгғҶгӮҜгғҒгғЈгӮ’жҺЎз”ЁгҒ—гҖҒеҠ№зҺҮзҡ„гҒӘгғҲгғ¬гғјгғӢгғігӮ°гӮ’е®ҹзҸҫгҖӮImageNetгҒ§1.61гҒ®FIDгӮ’йҒ”жҲҗгҒ—гҖҒгғҶгӮӯгӮ№гғҲгҒӢгӮүз”»еғҸгҒёгҒ®з”ҹжҲҗгҒ«гӮӮжӢЎејөгҖӮGenEvalгҒ§0.74гҖҒDPG-benchгҒ§83.5гӮ’иЁҳйҢІгҒ—гҖҒж—ўеӯҳгғўгғҮгғ«гӮ’дёҠеӣһгӮӢжҖ§иғҪгӮ’зӨәгҒ—гҒҹгҖӮ

paper-entry.title: [Paper Note] Kandinsky 5.0: A Family of Foundation Models for Image and Video Generation, Vladimir Arkhipkin+, arXiv'25, 2025.11
paper-entry.tags: ComputerVision, EfficiencyImprovement, Supervised-FineTuning (SFT), ReinforcementLearning, FoundationModel, DiffusionModel, TextToImageGeneration, SmallModel, VideoGeneration/Understandings, VisionLanguageModel
paper-entry.issue-body: # URL - https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.14993 # Authors - Vladimir Arkhipkin - Vladimir Korviakov - Nikolai Gerasimenko - Denis Parkhomenko - Viacheslav Vasilev - Alexey Letunovskiy - Maria Kovaleva - Nikolai Vaulin - Ivan Kirillov - Lev Novitskiy - Denis Koposov - Nikita Kiselev - Alexander Varlamov - Dmitrii Mikhailov - Vladimir Polovnikov - Andrey Shutkin - Ilya Vasiliev - Julia Agafonova - Anastasiia Kargapoltseva - Anna Dmitrienko - Anastasia Maltseva - Anna Averchenkova - Olga Kim - Tatiana Nikulina - Denis Dimitrov # Abstract - This report introduces Kandinsky 5.0, a family of state-of-the-art foundation models for high-resolution image and 10-second video synthesis. The framework comprises three core line-up of models: Kandinsky 5.0 Image Lite - a line-up of 6B parameter image generation models, Kandinsky 5.0 Video Lite - a fast and lightweight 2B parameter text-to-video and image-to-video models, and Kandinsky 5.0 Video Pro - 19B parameter models that achieves superior video generation quality. We provide a comprehensive review of the data curation lifecycle - including collection, processing, filtering and clustering - for the multi-stage training pipeline that involves extensive pre-training and incorporates quality-enhancement techniques such as self-supervised fine-tuning (SFT) and reinforcement learning (RL)-based post-training. We also present novel architectural, training, and inference optimizations that enable Kandinsky 5.0 to achieve high generation speeds and state-of-the-art performance across various tasks, as demonstrated by human evaluation. As a large-scale, publicly available generative framework, Kandinsky 5.0 leverages the full potential of its pre-training and subsequent stages to be adapted for a wide range of generative applications. We hope that this report, together with the release of our open-source code and training checkpoints, will substantially advance the development and accessibility of high-quality generative models for the research community. # Translation (by gpt-4o-mini) - жң¬е ұе‘ҠгҒ§гҒҜгҖҒKandinsky 5.0гӮ’зҙ№д»ӢгҒ—гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮгҒ“гӮҢгҒҜгҖҒй«ҳи§ЈеғҸеәҰгҒ®з”»еғҸгҒҠгӮҲгҒі10з§’гҒ®еӢ•з”»еҗҲжҲҗгҒ®гҒҹгӮҒгҒ®жңҖе…Ҳз«ҜгҒ®еҹәзӣӨгғўгғҮгғ«гҒ®гғ•гӮЎгғҹгғӘгғјгҒ§гҒҷгҖӮгҒ“гҒ®гғ•гғ¬гғјгғ гғҜгғјгӮҜгҒҜгҖҒ3гҒӨгҒ®гӮігӮўгғўгғҮгғ«гғ©гӮӨгғігӮўгғғгғ—гҒ§ж§ӢжҲҗгҒ•гӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮKandinsky 5.0 Image Lite - 6Bгғ‘гғ©гғЎгғјгӮҝгҒ®з”»еғҸз”ҹжҲҗгғўгғҮгғ«гҒ®гғ©гӮӨгғігӮўгғғгғ—гҖҒKandinsky 5.0 Video Lite - й«ҳйҖҹгҒ§и»ҪйҮҸгҒӘ2Bгғ‘гғ©гғЎгғјгӮҝгҒ®гғҶгӮӯгӮ№гғҲгҒӢгӮүеӢ•з”»гҖҒз”»еғҸгҒӢгӮүеӢ•з”»гҒёгҒ®гғўгғҮгғ«гҖҒгҒқгҒ—гҒҰKandinsky 5.0 Video Pro - е„ӘгӮҢгҒҹеӢ•з”»з”ҹжҲҗе“ҒиіӘгӮ’е®ҹзҸҫгҒҷгӮӢ19Bгғ‘гғ©гғЎгғјгӮҝгҒ®гғўгғҮгғ«гҒ§гҒҷгҖӮз§ҒгҒҹгҒЎгҒҜгҖҒеҸҺйӣҶгҖҒеҮҰзҗҶгҖҒгғ•гӮЈгғ«гӮҝгғӘгғігӮ°гҖҒгӮҜгғ©гӮ№гӮҝгғӘгғігӮ°гӮ’еҗ«гӮҖгғҮгғјгӮҝгӮӯгғҘгғ¬гғјгӮ·гғ§гғігғ©гӮӨгғ•гӮөгӮӨгӮҜгғ«гҒ®еҢ…жӢ¬зҡ„гҒӘгғ¬гғ“гғҘгғјгӮ’жҸҗдҫӣгҒ—гҖҒеәғзҜ„гҒӘдәӢеүҚгғҲгғ¬гғјгғӢгғігӮ°гӮ’еҗ«гӮҖеӨҡж®өйҡҺгғҲгғ¬гғјгғӢгғігӮ°гғ‘гӮӨгғ—гғ©гӮӨгғігҒ®гҒҹгӮҒгҒ®е“ҒиіӘеҗ‘дёҠжҠҖиЎ“пјҲиҮӘе·ұж•ҷеё«гҒӮгӮҠгғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғігғҒгғҘгғјгғӢгғігӮ°пјҲSFTпјүгӮ„еј·еҢ–еӯҰзҝ’пјҲRLпјүгҒ«еҹәгҒҘгҒҸгғқгӮ№гғҲгғҲгғ¬гғјгғӢгғігӮ°гҒӘгҒ©пјүгӮ’зө„гҒҝиҫјгӮ“гҒ§гҒ„гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮгҒҫгҒҹгҖҒKandinsky 5.0гҒҢй«ҳгҒ„з”ҹжҲҗйҖҹеәҰгҒЁжңҖе…Ҳз«ҜгҒ®гғ‘гғ•гӮ©гғјгғһгғігӮ№гӮ’йҒ”жҲҗгҒҷгӮӢгҒҹгӮҒгҒ®ж–°гҒ—гҒ„гӮўгғјгӮӯгғҶгӮҜгғҒгғЈгҖҒгғҲгғ¬гғјгғӢгғігӮ°гҖҒжҺЁи«–гҒ®жңҖйҒ©еҢ–гӮ’жҸҗзӨәгҒ—гҖҒдәәй–“и©•дҫЎгҒ«гӮҲгҒЈгҒҰгҒқгҒ®еҠ№жһңгӮ’зӨәгҒ—гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮеӨ§иҰҸжЁЎгҒ§е…¬й–ӢеҸҜиғҪгҒӘз”ҹжҲҗгғ•гғ¬гғјгғ гғҜгғјгӮҜгҒЁгҒ—гҒҰгҖҒKandinsky 5.0гҒҜгҖҒгҒқгҒ®дәӢеүҚгғҲгғ¬гғјгғӢгғігӮ°гҒЁгҒқгҒ®еҫҢгҒ®ж®өйҡҺгҒ®е®Ңе…ЁгҒӘжҪңеңЁиғҪеҠӣгӮ’жҙ»з”ЁгҒ—гҖҒе№…еәғгҒ„з”ҹжҲҗгӮўгғ—гғӘгӮұгғјгӮ·гғ§гғігҒ«йҒ©еҝңгҒ§гҒҚгӮӢгӮҲгҒҶгҒ«гҒ—гҒҰгҒ„гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮгҒ“гҒ®е ұе‘ҠжӣёгҒЁгҖҒз§ҒгҒҹгҒЎгҒ®гӮӘгғјгғ—гғігӮҪгғјгӮ№гӮігғјгғүгҒҠгӮҲгҒігғҲгғ¬гғјгғӢгғігӮ°гғҒгӮ§гғғгӮҜгғқгӮӨгғігғҲгҒ®гғӘгғӘгғјгӮ№гҒҢгҖҒз ”з©¶гӮігғҹгғҘгғӢгғҶгӮЈгҒ«гҒҠгҒ‘гӮӢй«ҳе“ҒиіӘз”ҹжҲҗгғўгғҮгғ«гҒ®й–ӢзҷәгҒЁгӮўгӮҜгӮ»гӮ№гҒ®еҗ‘дёҠгҒ«еӨ§гҒҚгҒҸиІўзҢ®гҒҷгӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгӮ’жңҹеҫ…гҒ—гҒҰгҒ„гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮ # Summary (by gpt-4o-mini) - Kandinsky 5.0гҒҜгҖҒй«ҳи§ЈеғҸеәҰз”»еғҸгҒЁ10з§’еӢ•з”»еҗҲжҲҗгҒ®гҒҹгӮҒгҒ®жңҖе…Ҳз«ҜгғўгғҮгғ«гҒ§гҖҒ3гҒӨгҒ®гӮігӮўгғўгғҮгғ«пјҲImage LiteгҖҒVideo LiteгҖҒVideo ProпјүгҒӢгӮүж§ӢжҲҗгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢгҖӮгғҮгғјгӮҝгӮӯгғҘгғ¬гғјгӮ·гғ§гғігғ©гӮӨгғ•гӮөгӮӨгӮҜгғ«гҒ®гғ¬гғ“гғҘгғјгӮ„гҖҒиҮӘе·ұж•ҷеё«гҒӮгӮҠгғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғігғҒгғҘгғјгғӢгғігӮ°гӮ„еј·еҢ–еӯҰзҝ’гӮ’з”ЁгҒ„гҒҹе“ҒиіӘеҗ‘дёҠжҠҖиЎ“гӮ’еҸ–гӮҠе…ҘгӮҢгҖҒй«ҳгҒ„з”ҹжҲҗйҖҹеәҰгҒЁгғ‘гғ•гӮ©гғјгғһгғігӮ№гӮ’е®ҹзҸҫгҖӮгӮӘгғјгғ—гғігӮҪгғјгӮ№гӮігғјгғүгҒЁгғҲгғ¬гғјгғӢгғігӮ°гғҒгӮ§гғғгӮҜгғқгӮӨгғігғҲгҒ®жҸҗдҫӣгҒ«гӮҲгӮҠгҖҒз ”з©¶гӮігғҹгғҘгғӢгғҶгӮЈгҒ®зҷәеұ•гҒ«еҜ„дёҺгҒҷгӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгӮ’зӣ®жҢҮгҒҷгҖӮ

paper-entry.title: [Paper Note] Uni-MoE-2.0-Omni: Scaling Language-Centric Omnimodal Large Model with Advanced MoE, Training and Data, Yunxin Li+, arXiv'25, 2025.11
paper-entry.tags: ComputerVision, Pretraining, NLP, LanguageModel, MultiModal, SpeechProcessing, DiffusionModel, PositionalEncoding, MoE(Mixture-of-Experts), read-later, Selected Papers/Blogs, DataMixture, 2D (Image), Routing, UMM, 4D (Video), Omni, KeyPoint Notes, audio, text
paper-entry.issue-body: # URL - https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.12609 # Authors - Yunxin Li - Xinyu Chen - Shenyuan Jiang - Haoyuan Shi - Zhenyu Liu - Xuanyu Zhang - Nanhao Deng - Zhenran Xu - Yicheng Ma - Meishan Zhang - Baotian Hu - Min Zhang # Abstract - We present Uni-MoE 2.0 from the Lychee family. As a fully open-source omnimodal large model (OLM), it substantially advances Lychee's Uni-MoE series in language-centric multimodal understanding, reasoning, and generating. Based on the Qwen2.5-7B dense architecture, we build Uni-MoE-2.0-Omni from scratch through three core contributions: dynamic-capacity Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) design, a progressive training strategy enhanced with an iterative reinforcement strategy, and a carefully curated multimodal data matching technique. It is capable of omnimodal understanding, as well as generating images, text, and speech. Architecturally, our new MoE framework balances computational efficiency and capability for 10 cross-modal inputs using shared, routed, and null experts, while our Omni-Modality 3D RoPE ensures spatio-temporal cross-modality alignment in the self-attention layer. For training, following cross-modal pretraining, we use a progressive supervised fine-tuning strategy that activates modality-specific experts and is enhanced by balanced data composition and an iterative GSPO-DPO method to stabilise RL training and improve reasoning. Data-wise, the base model, trained on approximately 75B tokens of open-source multimodal data, is equipped with special speech and image generation tokens, allowing it to learn these generative tasks by conditioning its outputs on linguistic cues. Extensive evaluation across 85 benchmarks demonstrates that our model achieves SOTA or highly competitive performance against leading OLMs, surpassing Qwen2.5-Omni (trained with 1.2T tokens) on over 50 of 76 benchmarks. Key strengths include video understanding (+7% avg. of 8), omnimodallity understanding (+7% avg. of 4), and audiovisual reasoning (+4%). It also advances long-form speech processing (reducing WER by 4.2%) and leads in low-level image processing and controllable generation across 5 metrics. # Translation (by gpt-4o-mini) - з§ҒгҒҹгҒЎгҒҜгҖҒLycheeгғ•гӮЎгғҹгғӘгғјгҒӢгӮүUni-MoE 2.0гӮ’зҷәиЎЁгҒ—гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮгҒ“гӮҢгҒҜе®Ңе…ЁгҒ«гӮӘгғјгғ—гғігӮҪгғјгӮ№гҒ®гӮӘгғ гғӢгғўгғјгғҖгғ«еӨ§иҰҸжЁЎгғўгғҮгғ«пјҲOLMпјүгҒ§гҒӮгӮҠгҖҒиЁҖиӘһдёӯеҝғгҒ®гғһгғ«гғҒгғўгғјгғҖгғ«зҗҶи§ЈгҖҒжҺЁи«–гҖҒз”ҹжҲҗгҒ«гҒҠгҒ„гҒҰLycheeгҒ®Uni-MoEгӮ·гғӘгғјгӮәгӮ’еӨ§е№…гҒ«йҖІеұ•гҒ•гҒӣгҒҫгҒҷгҖӮQwen2.5-7BгҒ®еҜҶгҒӘгӮўгғјгӮӯгғҶгӮҜгғҒгғЈгҒ«еҹәгҒҘгҒҚгҖҒз§ҒгҒҹгҒЎгҒҜUni-MoE-2.0-OmniгӮ’гӮјгғӯгҒӢгӮүж§ӢзҜүгҒ—гҒҫгҒ—гҒҹгҖӮгҒ“гӮҢгҒ«гҒҜгҖҒеӢ•зҡ„е®№йҮҸгҒ®Mixture-of-ExpertsпјҲMoEпјүиЁӯиЁҲгҖҒеҸҚеҫ©еј·еҢ–жҲҰз•ҘгӮ’еј·еҢ–гҒ—гҒҹйҖІиЎҢзҡ„гғҲгғ¬гғјгғӢгғігӮ°жҲҰз•ҘгҖҒгҒқгҒ—гҒҰж…ҺйҮҚгҒ«гӮӯгғҘгғ¬гғјгӮ·гғ§гғігҒ•гӮҢгҒҹгғһгғ«гғҒгғўгғјгғҖгғ«гғҮгғјгӮҝгғһгғғгғҒгғігӮ°жҠҖиЎ“гҒЁгҒ„гҒҶ3гҒӨгҒ®гӮігӮўгҒӘиІўзҢ®гҒҢеҗ«гҒҫгӮҢгҒҫгҒҷгҖӮгҒ“гҒ®гғўгғҮгғ«гҒҜгҖҒгӮӘгғ гғӢгғўгғјгғҖгғ«гҒӘзҗҶи§ЈгҒ гҒ‘гҒ§гҒӘгҒҸгҖҒз”»еғҸгҖҒгғҶгӮӯгӮ№гғҲгҖҒйҹіеЈ°гҒ®з”ҹжҲҗгӮӮеҸҜиғҪгҒ§гҒҷгҖӮгӮўгғјгӮӯгғҶгӮҜгғҒгғЈзҡ„гҒ«гҒҜгҖҒж–°гҒ—гҒ„MoEгғ•гғ¬гғјгғ гғҜгғјгӮҜгҒҜгҖҒе…ұжңүгҖҒгғ«гғјгғҶгӮЈгғігӮ°гҖҒз„ЎеҠ№гҒӘгӮЁгӮӯгӮ№гғ‘гғјгғҲгӮ’дҪҝз”ЁгҒ—гҒҰ10гҒ®гӮҜгғӯгӮ№гғўгғјгғҖгғ«е…ҘеҠӣгҒ«еҜҫгҒҷгӮӢиЁҲз®—еҠ№зҺҮгҒЁиғҪеҠӣгҒ®гғҗгғ©гғігӮ№гӮ’еҸ–гҒЈгҒҰгҒ„гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮгҒҫгҒҹгҖҒз§ҒгҒҹгҒЎгҒ®гӮӘгғ гғӢгғўгғҖгғӘгғҶгӮЈ3D RoPEгҒҜгҖҒиҮӘе·ұжіЁж„ҸеұӨгҒ«гҒҠгҒ‘гӮӢз©әй–“зҡ„гғ»жҷӮй–“зҡ„гӮҜгғӯгӮ№гғўгғјгғҖгғӘгғҶгӮЈгҒ®ж•ҙеҗҲжҖ§гӮ’зўәдҝқгҒ—гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮгғҲгғ¬гғјгғӢгғігӮ°гҒ«гҒҠгҒ„гҒҰгҒҜгҖҒгӮҜгғӯгӮ№гғўгғјгғҖгғ«гҒ®дәӢеүҚгғҲгғ¬гғјгғӢгғігӮ°гҒ«з¶ҡгҒҚгҖҒгғўгғҖгғӘгғҶгӮЈзү№жңүгҒ®гӮЁгӮӯгӮ№гғ‘гғјгғҲгӮ’жҙ»жҖ§еҢ–гҒҷгӮӢйҖІиЎҢзҡ„гҒӘж•ҷеё«гҒӮгӮҠгғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғігғҒгғҘгғјгғӢгғігӮ°жҲҰз•ҘгӮ’дҪҝз”ЁгҒ—гҖҒгғҗгғ©гғігӮ№гҒ®еҸ–гӮҢгҒҹгғҮгғјгӮҝж§ӢжҲҗгҒЁеҸҚеҫ©зҡ„гҒӘGSPO-DPOжүӢжі•гҒ«гӮҲгҒЈгҒҰRLгғҲгғ¬гғјгғӢгғігӮ°гӮ’е®үе®ҡеҢ–гҒ—гҖҒжҺЁи«–гӮ’ж”№е–„гҒ—гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮгғҮгғјгӮҝйқўгҒ§гҒҜгҖҒзҙ„75BгғҲгғјгӮҜгғігҒ®гӮӘгғјгғ—гғігӮҪгғјгӮ№гғһгғ«гғҒгғўгғјгғҖгғ«гғҮгғјгӮҝгҒ§гғҲгғ¬гғјгғӢгғігӮ°гҒ•гӮҢгҒҹгғҷгғјгӮ№гғўгғҮгғ«гҒҜгҖҒзү№еҲҘгҒӘйҹіеЈ°гҒҠгӮҲгҒіз”»еғҸз”ҹжҲҗгғҲгғјгӮҜгғігӮ’иЈ…еӮҷгҒ—гҒҰгҒҠгӮҠгҖҒиЁҖиӘһзҡ„жүӢгҒҢгҒӢгӮҠгҒ«еҹәгҒҘгҒ„гҒҰеҮәеҠӣгӮ’жқЎд»¶д»ҳгҒ‘гӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгҒ§гҖҒгҒ“гӮҢгӮүгҒ®з”ҹжҲҗгӮҝгӮ№гӮҜгӮ’еӯҰзҝ’гҒ—гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮ85гҒ®гғҷгғігғҒгғһгғјгӮҜгҒ«гӮҸгҒҹгӮӢеәғзҜ„гҒӘи©•дҫЎгҒ«гӮҲгӮҠгҖҒз§ҒгҒҹгҒЎгҒ®гғўгғҮгғ«гҒҜSOTAгҒҫгҒҹгҒҜз«¶дәүеҠӣгҒ®гҒӮгӮӢгғ‘гғ•гӮ©гғјгғһгғігӮ№гӮ’йҒ”жҲҗгҒ—гҖҒ76гҒ®гғҷгғігғҒгғһгғјгӮҜгҒ®гҒҶгҒЎ50д»ҘдёҠгҒ§1.2TгғҲгғјгӮҜгғігҒ§гғҲгғ¬гғјгғӢгғігӮ°гҒ•гӮҢгҒҹQwen2.5-OmniгӮ’дёҠеӣһгҒЈгҒҰгҒ„гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮдё»гҒӘеј·гҒҝгҒ«гҒҜгҖҒгғ“гғҮгӮӘзҗҶи§ЈпјҲе№іеқҮ+7%гҒ®8пјүгҖҒгӮӘгғ гғӢгғўгғјгғҖгғӘгғҶгӮЈзҗҶи§ЈпјҲе№іеқҮ+7%гҒ®4пјүгҖҒгҒҠгӮҲгҒіиҰ–иҒҙиҰҡжҺЁи«–пјҲ+4%пјүгҒҢеҗ«гҒҫгӮҢгҒҫгҒҷгҖӮгҒҫгҒҹгҖҒй•·ж–ҮйҹіеЈ°еҮҰзҗҶгӮ’йҖІеұ•гҒ•гҒӣпјҲWERгӮ’4.2%еүҠжёӣпјүгҖҒ5гҒӨгҒ®жҢҮжЁҷгҒ«гӮҸгҒҹгҒЈгҒҰдҪҺгғ¬гғҷгғ«гҒ®з”»еғҸеҮҰзҗҶгҒЁеҲ¶еҫЎеҸҜиғҪгҒӘз”ҹжҲҗгҒ§гғӘгғјгғүгҒ—гҒҰгҒ„гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮ # Summary (by gpt-4o-mini) - Uni-MoE 2.0гҒҜгҖҒLycheeгғ•гӮЎгғҹгғӘгғјгҒӢгӮүзҷәиЎЁгҒ•гӮҢгҒҹгӮӘгғјгғ—гғігӮҪгғјгӮ№гҒ®гӮӘгғ гғӢгғўгғјгғҖгғ«еӨ§иҰҸжЁЎгғўгғҮгғ«гҒ§гҖҒиЁҖиӘһдёӯеҝғгҒ®гғһгғ«гғҒгғўгғјгғҖгғ«зҗҶи§ЈгҒЁз”ҹжҲҗгӮ’еӨ§е№…гҒ«еҗ‘дёҠгҒ•гҒӣгӮӢгҖӮеӢ•зҡ„е®№йҮҸгҒ®Mixture-of-ExpertsиЁӯиЁҲгӮ„йҖІиЎҢзҡ„гғҲгғ¬гғјгғӢгғігӮ°жҲҰз•ҘгӮ’жҺЎз”ЁгҒ—гҖҒз”»еғҸгҖҒгғҶгӮӯгӮ№гғҲгҖҒйҹіеЈ°гҒ®з”ҹжҲҗгҒҢеҸҜиғҪгҖӮзҙ„75BгғҲгғјгӮҜгғігҒ®гғҮгғјгӮҝгҒ§гғҲгғ¬гғјгғӢгғігӮ°гҒ•гӮҢгҖҒ85гҒ®гғҷгғігғҒгғһгғјгӮҜгҒ§з«¶дәүеҠӣгҒ®гҒӮгӮӢжҖ§иғҪгӮ’зӨәгҒ—гҖҒзү№гҒ«гғ“гғҮгӮӘзҗҶи§ЈгӮ„гӮӘгғ гғӢгғўгғјгғҖгғӘгғҶгӮЈзҗҶи§ЈгҒ§е„ӘгӮҢгҒҹзөҗжһңгӮ’йҒ”жҲҗгҖӮ

maintainer-comment-text.p: pj pageгӮ’гҒҝгҒҹж„ҹгҒҳгҖҒгӮўгғјгӮӯгғҶгӮҜгғҒгғЈгҒҜдёӢиЁҳгҒ§гҖҒгғўгғҖгғӘгғҶгӮЈгҒ”гҒЁгҒ®гӮЁгғігӮігғјгғҖгӮ’з”Ёж„ҸгҒ—гғҲгғјгӮҜгғіеҢ–гҒ—еҗҢгҒҳз©әй–“дёҠгҒ§еҗ„гғўгғҖгғӘгғҶгӮЈгӮ’еӯҰзҝ’гҒҷгӮӢUnified Multi ModalгғўгғҮгғ«гҒЁгҒӘгҒЈгҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгҖӮMoEгӮўгғјгӮӯгғҶгӮҜгғҒгғЈгӮ’жҺЎз”ЁгҒ—гғўгғҖгғӘгғҶгӮЈгҒ”гҒЁгҒ®expertгҒЁе…ұжңүгҒ®expertгҖҒnull expert(гғ‘гғ©гғЎгғјгӮҝгӮӮеҝ…иҰҒгҒЁгҒӣгҒҡдҪ•гӮӮеҮҰзҗҶгҒ—гҒӘгҒ„expertгҒ§гӮўгғјгӮӯгғҶгӮҜгғҒгғЈгӮ’MoEгҒӢгӮүеӨүгҒҲгҒҡгҒ«дёҚиҰҒгҒӘиЁҲз®—гӮ’жҺ’йҷӨгҒ—гҒҰеҠ№зҺҮгӮ’еҗ‘дёҠеҸҜиғҪ)гӮ’з”Ёж„ҸгҒ—гғ«гғјгӮҝгҒ§еҲ¶еҫЎгҒҷгӮӢгҖӮгҒҫгҒҹгҖҒspeechгӮ„videoгҒӘгҒ©гҒ®жҷӮзі»еҲ—жҖ§гҒ«еҜҫеҮҰгҒҷгӮӢгҒҹгӮҒгҒ«RoPEгӮ’3ж¬Ўе…ғгҒ«жӢЎејөгҒ—гҒҹPEгӮ’з”ЁгҒ„гҒҰгҖҒеҗ„гғўгғҖгғӘгғҶгӮЈгҒҢгӮ·гғјгғ гғ¬гӮ№гҒ«alignmentгӮ’гҒЁгӮҢгӮӢгӮҲгҒҶгҒ«гҒ—гҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгҖӮ дәӢеүҚеӯҰзҝ’гҒ§гҒҜгҒҫгҒҡгғҶгӮӯгӮ№гғҲгӮ’дёӯеҝғгҒЁгҒ—гҒҹгӮҜгғӯгӮ№гғўгғјгғҖгғ«гҒӘеӯҰзҝ’гӮ’гҒҷгӮӢгҖӮгҒҹгҒЁгҒҲгҒ°гҖҒimage/audio/video-textгӮҝгӮ№гӮҜгҒ§еӯҰзҝ’гӮ’гҒҷгӮӢгҖӮгҒ“гҒ®гғ•гӮ§гғјгӮәгҒ§еҗ„гғўгғҖгғӘгғҶгӮЈгӮ’гғҶгӮӯгӮ№гғҲгҒ®semantic spaceгҒ«еҶҷеғҸгҒҷгӮӢиғҪеҠӣгӮ’йҚӣгҒҲгӮӢ(Figure5 left)гҖӮ гҒқгҒ®еҫҢSFTгҒ§еҗ„гғўгғҖгғӘгғҶгӮЈгҒ«зү№еҢ–гҒ—гҒҹexpertгӮ’еӯҰзҝ’гҒҷгӮӢгҖӮгҒ“гҒ“гҒ§гҒҜж®өйҡҺзҡ„гҒ«SFTгӮ’е®ҹж–ҪгҒ—гҖҒгҒҫгҒҡгҒҫгҒҡAudio, VisualгҒ®expertгӮ’еҗҢжҷӮгҒ«warmupгҒ—гҖҒгҒқгҒ®еҫҢTextгҒ®expertsгӮ’иҝҪеҠ гҒ—гҒҰж¬ЎгҒ®гӮўгғӢгғјгғӘгғігӮ°гғ•гӮ§гғјгӮәгӮ’еӯҰзҝ’гҒ—гҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгӮҲгҒҶгҒ«иҰӢгҒҲгӮӢгҖӮ з¶ҡгҒ„гҒҰгӮўгғӢгғјгғӘгғігӮ°гғ•гӮ§гғјгӮәгҒЁгҒ—гҒҰгҖҒж§ҳгҖ…гҒӘгғўгғҖгғӘгғҶгӮЈгҒ®гғҮгғјгӮҝгӮ’гғҗгғ©гғігӮ№гҒ•гҒӣгҒҰDataMixtureгҒ—гҖҒеҫҗгҖ…гҒ«еӯҰзҝ’зҺҮгӮ’дёӢгҒ’гҒӘгҒҢгӮүзү№е®ҡгҒ®гӮҝгӮ№гӮҜгӮ„гғўгғҖгғӘгғҶгӮЈгҒ«зү№еҢ–гҒӣгҒҡгҖҒе…ЁдҪ“гҒ®жҖ§иғҪгҒҢеҗ‘дёҠгҒҷгӮӢгӮҲгҒҶгҒ«еӯҰзҝ’гҒҷгӮӢгҖӮгҒқгҒ®еҫҢгҖҒlong sequenceгҒ®reasoningгҒ®иғҪеҠӣгӮ’еҗ‘дёҠгҒ•гҒӣгӮӢгҒҹгӮҒгҒ«GSPO-DPOгӮ’iterativeгҒ«йҒ©з”ЁгҒҷгӮӢгҖӮDPOгҒ®е—ңеҘҪгғҮгғјгӮҝгҒҜLLM as a JudgeгҒ§гғӯгғјгғ«гӮўгӮҰгғҲгҒ«еҹәгҒҘгҒ„гҒҰж§ӢжҲҗгҒҷгӮӢгҖҒ гҒЁгҒ„гҒҶж„ҹгҒҳгӮүгҒ—гҒ„гҖӮ Table2гҒ«DataMixtureгҒ®жҜ”зҺҮгҒҢгҒӢгҒӢгӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгҒ—гҖҒеҗ„зЁ®гғҮгғјгӮҝгҒ®жҰӮиҰҒгӮӮ3.2зҜҖгҒ«гҒӢгҒӢгӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгӮҲгҒҶгҒ«иҰӢгҒҲгӮӢгҖӮзҙ жҷҙгӮүгҒ—гҒ„гҖӮ

paper-entry.title: [Paper Note] Time-to-Move: Training-Free Motion Controlled Video Generation via Dual-Clock Denoising, Assaf Singer+, arXiv'25, 2025.11
paper-entry.tags: ComputerVision, Controllable, DiffusionModel, VideoGeneration/Understandings
paper-entry.issue-body: # URL - https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.08633 # Authors - Assaf Singer - Noam Rotstein - Amir Mann - Ron Kimmel - Or Litany # Abstract - Diffusion-based video generation can create realistic videos, yet existing image- and text-based conditioning fails to offer precise motion control. Prior methods for motion-conditioned synthesis typically require model-specific fine-tuning, which is computationally expensive and restrictive. We introduce Time-to-Move (TTM), a training-free, plug-and-play framework for motion- and appearance-controlled video generation with image-to-video (I2V) diffusion models. Our key insight is to use crude reference animations obtained through user-friendly manipulations such as cut-and-drag or depth-based reprojection. Motivated by SDEdit's use of coarse layout cues for image editing, we treat the crude animations as coarse motion cues and adapt the mechanism to the video domain. We preserve appearance with image conditioning and introduce dual-clock denoising, a region-dependent strategy that enforces strong alignment in motion-specified regions while allowing flexibility elsewhere, balancing fidelity to user intent with natural dynamics. This lightweight modification of the sampling process incurs no additional training or runtime cost and is compatible with any backbone. Extensive experiments on object and camera motion benchmarks show that TTM matches or exceeds existing training-based baselines in realism and motion control. Beyond this, TTM introduces a unique capability: precise appearance control through pixel-level conditioning, exceeding the limits of text-only prompting. Visit our project page for video examples and code: https://time-to-move.github.io/. # Translation (by gpt-4o-mini) - paper-entry.title: [Paper Note] TiDAR: Think in Diffusion, Talk in Autoregression, Jingyu Liu+, arXiv'25, 2025.11
paper-entry.tags: EfficiencyImprovement, NLP, LanguageModel, DiffusionModel, Decoding, read-later, Selected Papers/Blogs
paper-entry.issue-body: # URL - https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.08923 # Authors - Jingyu Liu - Xin Dong - Zhifan Ye - Rishabh Mehta - Yonggan Fu - Vartika Singh - Jan Kautz - Ce Zhang - Pavlo Molchanov # Abstract - Diffusion language models hold the promise of fast parallel generation, while autoregressive (AR) models typically excel in quality due to their causal structure aligning naturally with language modeling. This raises a fundamental question: can we achieve a synergy with high throughput, higher GPU utilization, and AR level quality? Existing methods fail to effectively balance these two aspects, either prioritizing AR using a weaker model for sequential drafting (speculative decoding), leading to lower drafting efficiency, or using some form of left-to-right (AR-like) decoding logic for diffusion, which still suffers from quality degradation and forfeits its potential parallelizability. We introduce TiDAR, a sequence-level hybrid architecture that drafts tokens (Thinking) in Diffusion and samples final outputs (Talking) AutoRegressively - all within a single forward pass using specially designed structured attention masks. This design exploits the free GPU compute density, achieving a strong balance between drafting and verification capacity. Moreover, TiDAR is designed to be serving-friendly (low overhead) as a standalone model. We extensively evaluate TiDAR against AR models, speculative decoding, and diffusion variants across generative and likelihood tasks at 1.5B and 8B scales. Thanks to the parallel drafting and sampling as well as exact KV cache support, TiDAR outperforms speculative decoding in measured throughput and surpasses diffusion models like Dream and Llada in both efficiency and quality. Most notably, TiDAR is the first architecture to close the quality gap with AR models while delivering 4.71x to 5.91x more tokens per second. # Translation (by gpt-4o-mini) - жӢЎж•ЈиЁҖиӘһгғўгғҮгғ«гҒҜгҖҒй«ҳйҖҹгҒӘдёҰеҲ—з”ҹжҲҗгҒ®еҸҜиғҪжҖ§гӮ’з§ҳгӮҒгҒҰгҒ„гҒҫгҒҷгҒҢгҖҒиҮӘе·ұеӣһеё°пјҲARпјүгғўгғҮгғ«гҒҜйҖҡеёёгҖҒеӣ жһңж§ӢйҖ гҒҢиЁҖиӘһгғўгғҮгғӘгғігӮ°гҒЁиҮӘз„¶гҒ«дёҖиҮҙгҒҷгӮӢгҒҹгӮҒгҖҒе“ҒиіӘгҒ«гҒҠгҒ„гҒҰе„ӘгӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮгҒ“гӮҢгҒ«гӮҲгӮҠгҖҒж №жң¬зҡ„гҒӘз–‘е•ҸгҒҢз”ҹгҒҳгҒҫгҒҷпјҡй«ҳгӮ№гғ«гғјгғ—гғғгғҲгҖҒй«ҳгҒ„GPUеҲ©з”ЁзҺҮгҖҒгҒқгҒ—гҒҰARгғ¬гғҷгғ«гҒ®е“ҒиіӘгӮ’дёЎз«ӢгҒ•гҒӣгӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгҒҜеҸҜиғҪгҒ§гҒ—гӮҮгҒҶгҒӢпјҹж—ўеӯҳгҒ®жүӢжі•гҒҜгҖҒгҒ“гӮҢгӮүдәҢгҒӨгҒ®еҒҙйқўгҒ®гғҗгғ©гғігӮ№гӮ’еҠ№жһңзҡ„гҒ«еҸ–гӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгҒҢгҒ§гҒҚгҒҡгҖҒARгӮ’е„Әе…ҲгҒҷгӮӢгҒҹгӮҒгҒ«ејұгҒ„гғўгғҮгғ«гӮ’дҪҝз”ЁгҒ—гҒҰйҖҗж¬Ўзҡ„гҒ«гғүгғ©гғ•гғҲгӮ’иЎҢгҒҶпјҲжҺЁжё¬зҡ„гғҮгӮігғјгғҮгӮЈгғігӮ°пјүгҒ“гҒЁгҒ§гғүгғ©гғ•гғҲеҠ№зҺҮгҒҢдҪҺдёӢгҒҷгӮӢгҒӢгҖҒжӢЎж•ЈгҒ«еҜҫгҒ—гҒҰе·ҰгҒӢгӮүеҸігҒёгҒ®пјҲARгҒ®гӮҲгҒҶгҒӘпјүгғҮгӮігғјгғҮгӮЈгғігӮ°гғӯгӮёгғғгӮҜгӮ’дҪҝз”ЁгҒҷгӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгҒ§гҖҒе“ҒиіӘгҒ®дҪҺдёӢгҒҢз”ҹгҒҳгҖҒдёҰеҲ—еҢ–гҒ®еҸҜиғҪжҖ§гӮ’еӨұгҒЈгҒҰгҒ„гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮз§ҒгҒҹгҒЎгҒҜгҖҒTiDARгҒЁгҒ„гҒҶгӮ·гғјгӮұгғігӮ№гғ¬гғҷгғ«гҒ®гғҸгӮӨгғ–гғӘгғғгғүгӮўгғјгӮӯгғҶгӮҜгғҒгғЈгӮ’жҸҗжЎҲгҒ—гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮгҒ“гӮҢгҒҜгҖҒзү№еҲҘгҒ«иЁӯиЁҲгҒ•гӮҢгҒҹж§ӢйҖ еҢ–гӮўгғҶгғігӮ·гғ§гғігғһгӮ№гӮҜгӮ’дҪҝз”ЁгҒ—гҒҰгҖҒжӢЎж•ЈгҒ«гҒҠгҒ„гҒҰгғҲгғјгӮҜгғігӮ’гғүгғ©гғ•гғҲпјҲThinkingпјүгҒ—гҖҒжңҖзөӮеҮәеҠӣгӮ’иҮӘе·ұеӣһеё°зҡ„гҒ«гӮөгғігғ—гғӘгғігӮ°пјҲTalkingпјүгҒҷгӮӢгӮӮгҒ®гҒ§гҖҒгҒҷгҒ№гҒҰгӮ’еҚҳдёҖгҒ®гғ•гӮ©гғҜгғјгғүгғ‘гӮ№еҶ…гҒ§е®ҹиЎҢгҒ—гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮгҒ“гҒ®иЁӯиЁҲгҒҜгҖҒGPUгҒ®иЁҲз®—еҜҶеәҰгӮ’жңҖеӨ§йҷҗгҒ«жҙ»з”ЁгҒ—гҖҒгғүгғ©гғ•гғҲгҒЁжӨңиЁјиғҪеҠӣгҒ®еј·еҠӣгҒӘгғҗгғ©гғігӮ№гӮ’е®ҹзҸҫгҒ—гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮгҒ•гӮүгҒ«гҖҒTiDARгҒҜгӮ№гӮҝгғігғүгӮўгғӯгғігғўгғҮгғ«гҒЁгҒ—гҒҰгӮөгғјгғ“гӮ№гҒ«йҒ©гҒ—гҒҹпјҲгӮӘгғјгғҗгғјгғҳгғғгғүгҒҢдҪҺгҒ„пјүиЁӯиЁҲгҒ«гҒӘгҒЈгҒҰгҒ„гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮз§ҒгҒҹгҒЎгҒҜгҖҒ1.5BгҒҠгӮҲгҒі8BгӮ№гӮұгғјгғ«гҒ§гҒ®з”ҹжҲҗгӮҝгӮ№гӮҜгҒЁе°ӨеәҰгӮҝгӮ№гӮҜгҒ«гҒҠгҒ„гҒҰгҖҒTiDARгӮ’ARгғўгғҮгғ«гҖҒжҺЁжё¬зҡ„гғҮгӮігғјгғҮгӮЈгғігӮ°гҖҒжӢЎж•ЈгҒ®гғҗгғӘгӮЁгғјгӮ·гғ§гғігҒЁеәғзҜ„гҒ«и©•дҫЎгҒ—гҒҫгҒ—гҒҹгҖӮдёҰеҲ—гғүгғ©гғ•гғҲгҒЁгӮөгғігғ—гғӘгғігӮ°гҖҒгҒ•гӮүгҒ«жӯЈзўәгҒӘKVгӮӯгғЈгғғгӮ·гғҘгӮөгғқгғјгғҲгҒ®гҒҠгҒӢгҒ’гҒ§гҖҒTiDARгҒҜжё¬е®ҡгҒ•гӮҢгҒҹгӮ№гғ«гғјгғ—гғғгғҲгҒ«гҒҠгҒ„гҒҰжҺЁжё¬зҡ„гғҮгӮігғјгғҮгӮЈгғігӮ°гӮ’дёҠеӣһгӮҠгҖҒеҠ№зҺҮгҒЁе“ҒиіӘгҒ®дёЎйқўгҒ§DreamгӮ„LladaгҒ®гӮҲгҒҶгҒӘжӢЎж•ЈгғўгғҮгғ«гӮ’и¶…гҒҲгҒҫгҒ—гҒҹгҖӮзү№гҒ«жіЁзӣ®гҒҷгҒ№гҒҚгҒҜгҖҒTiDARгҒҢARгғўгғҮгғ«гҒЁгҒ®е“ҒиіӘгӮ®гғЈгғғгғ—гӮ’еҲқгӮҒгҒҰеҹӢгӮҒгҒӘгҒҢгӮүгҖҒ1з§’гҒӮгҒҹгӮҠ4.71еҖҚгҒӢгӮү5.91еҖҚгҒ®гғҲгғјгӮҜгғігӮ’жҸҗдҫӣгҒҷгӮӢгӮўгғјгӮӯгғҶгӮҜгғҒгғЈгҒ§гҒӮгӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгҒ§гҒҷгҖӮ
